+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. DAVID CAMPBELL NAMED TO AZ BIG MEDIA'S 2026 "PEOPLE TO KNOW IN COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE" LIST

PHOENIX, Arizona (November 6, 2025) - PCL Buildings Inc. is pleased to announce that David Campbell, district manager for PCL Construction's Phoenix buildings office, has been named to AZ Big Media's "2026 People and Projects to Know in Commercial Real Estate" list. This annual recognition celebrates influential leaders who are shaping the future of Arizona's commercial real estate industry.

"This recognition highlights the momentum we're building in Arizona's construction market and our commitment to contributing positively to the region's future," says Campbell. "I'm proud to represent PCL and support the growth and innovation happening across Arizona."

Campbell was named as the leader of PCL's newest commercial buildings office in Phoenix this past spring, building upon a nearly 40-year legacy of excellence in water and civil construction across the Southwest. The Phoenix Buildings office marks a strategic milestone in PCL's commitment to the region, offering construction expertise across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, aviation, and commercial and retail development.

His appearance on AZ Big Media's list reflects Campbell's contributions to the industry over the course of his nearly 20-year construction career, having led a wide range of high-profile projects across the U.S. Campbell joined PCL in 2005, with early contributions to entertainment and hospitality developments in Orlando. He later served as senior project manager on the American Dream megamall in New Jersey and held several leadership roles at PCL's U.S. head office in Denver before his current role in Phoenix.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JGB-XRV3-RRRH-604J-00000-00WALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY - HIGHLIGHTS FROM BARTON MALOW COMPANY AND PEER COMPANIES2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:39ZLexisNexisSTOCKWALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY - HIGHLIGHTS FROM BARTON MALOW COMPANY AND PEER COMPANIES

DASHBOARD: WALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY

PermID5000066406Websitehttp://www.walbridge.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress777 Woodward Ave., Ste. 300 Detroit, MI 48226

ACTIVITIES:

Walbridge Aldinger Company, founded in 1916, is a construction management firm headquartered in Detroit, Michigan. With over 1,500 employees, it serves automotive, commercial, and industrial sectors, delivering projects like manufacturing plants and data centers. Generating over $2 billion annually, Walbridge is known for its lean construction practices and safety focus. In 2024, it expanded its data center and renewable energy project portfolio, capitalizing on tech infrastructure demand. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and employee development, with strong community engagement initiatives. Competing with Gilbane, Walbridge stands out for its automotive expertise and client-centric approach. Recent initiatives include investments in sustainable construction and digital project management tools, reinforcing its industry leadership.

+ WALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY [1 Press Release]

+ WALBRIDGE IMPROVES ECOVADIS SCORE, RANKING AMONG TOP-RATED COMPANIES IN 2025

Building on the foundation Walbridge has established, our sustainability journey continues to gain momentum. Our 2025 EcoVadis assessment score increased from 60 to 67, placing us in the 77th percentile among more than 150,000 companies rated worldwide. This year's results reflect meaningful progress across several key sustainability categories and reinforce our standing as one of the top-rated companies in our industry and among companies of similar size.

EcoVadis is a globally recognized platform for measuring sustainability performance across business operations. The assessment reviews 21 criteria across Environment, Labor and Human Rights, Ethics and Sustainable Procurement. We continue to leverage EcoVadis feedback to refine our sustainability strategies and strengthen areas for continued progress.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [4 Press Releases]

+ BARTON MALOW + SHERIDAN CONSTRUCTION SET TO BUILD NEW MACON ARENA

MACON, GEORGIA - Barton Malow, in association with Sheridan Construction, has been chosen as the Construction Manager for the new Macon Arena, a major project that will replace the current Macon Coliseum and serve as a key part of the region's expanding entertainment opportunities.

Located southeast of Atlanta in Macon-Bibb County, the planned facility is designed to host various multipurpose events like minor league hockey, basketball games, high school tournaments, concerts, conferences, trade shows, and more. The new arena will offer a top-tier guest experience, featuring premium seating options like luxury suites, club suites, and loge boxes, as well as modern fan amenities such as clubs, concessions, restrooms, locker rooms, and integrated technologies. Back-of-house and support spaces will ensure the venue functions as a state-of-the-art civic facility, offering performers, event planners, and organizations a great experience.

"This arena won't be just a venue, it will be a destination and major economic engine that creates opportunities, attracts visitors, and keeps our city competitive for years to come," said Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller.

As Macon's entertainment landscape continues to expand with the new Atrium Health Amphitheater and the recently renovated Macon City Auditorium, the new arena is poised to further elevate the region's growing lineup of elite venues.

"We're thrilled to partner with Sheridan Construction and bring Barton Malow's expertise to Macon-Bibb to deliver a top-tier arena. The new venue is set to become a central location in Georgia for sports, entertainment, and community gatherings," said Barton Malow Office Director Blake Boyd. "This project is an investment in Macon-Bibb County's future, and we're excited to support the local community every step of the way."

Adds Sheridan Construction President + CEO Christy Kovac. "It's a privilege for our firm to return to Macon's legacy of entertainment and civic pride, having built the original coliseum 57 years ago. Partnering with Barton Malow on this transformative new arena is both an honor and a responsibility. We believe this project will serve as a powerful economic and tourism engine, attracting visitors, creating opportunities, and enriching the lives of everyone in our community for generations to come."

Construction is planned to commence in summer 2026, with the project anticipated to reach completion in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY LUKE KNOCHEL PROMOTED TO VICE PRESIDENT OF PROJECT CONTROLS

Luke's project controls leadership role spans multiple geographic regions and consists of overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Barton Malow has announced the promotion of Luke Knochel to Vice President of Project Controls.

In his new role, Luke leads a multi‑disciplinary team responsible for core functions such as cost and schedule management, forecasting, project start‑up, accounts receivable and payable processes, and change management. His leadership spans multiple geographic regions, overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Luke started at Barton Malow in 2013 as a Cost Analyst and has served in various project controls and finance leadership roles throughout his tenure. He oversaw accounts payable and accounts receivable, change management, forecasting, reporting, and more on the Little Caesars Arena project, then went on to supervise Barton Malow's offices in Chicago, Columbus, and Nashville. After a promotion to Project Controls Manager overseeing the then-Midwest business unit, Luke transitioned into a corporate finance role managing the shared service department and implementing new accounts payable software.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY BUILDING FOR THE BRAND: CONSTRUCTING COLLEGE ATHLETIC FACILITIES IN THE NIL ERA

NIL athletic construction: Many modern facilities now include dedicated content creation zones where athletes can produce high-quality photos and videos for social media and sponsorship campaigns.

On July 1, 2021, the NCAA formally adopted a policy allowing college athletes to earn money for their name, image, and likeness (NIL).

At the time, one of my acquaintances, Matt Wilson, was serving as the Senior Director of Development for Athletics at Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University. He witnessed firsthand the immediate impact that NIL had on college athletics.

Today, Matt sees things from the other side of the table. After leaving Embry-Riddle in 2022, he spent two years as Executive Director of NIL Collective Operations + Development at Athlete Forward Initiative. Now he runs his own consulting group designed to provide leadership, fundraising, communication, and culture development training for college athletic departments.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW EARNS ECOVADIS BRONZE MEDAL FOR SUSTAINABILITY EXCELLENCE

Barton Malow has been awarded the EcoVadis Bronze Medal, placing the construction enterprise among the top 35% of companies worldwide for sustainability performance. This achievement underscores Barton Malow's leadership in green building practices, ethical business operations, and sustainable procurement.

"This recognition marks a significant milestone in our ongoing commitment to integrating sustainability principles across our business and reflects the progress we've made in partnership with our clients and stakeholders," said Senior Director of Sustainability Jill Katic.

EcoVadis, the world's most trusted provider of business sustainability ratings, evaluates companies on four key themes: Environment, Labor & Human Rights, Ethics, and Sustainable Procurement.

Barton Malow is committed to creating a positive impact on the planet, its people, and the communities in which it builds. To learn more about how Barton Malow is working towards its long-term environmental commitments, like reducing carbon emissions and implementing green building practices, explore the 2025 Sustainability Impact Report.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION U.S. CONSTRUCTION DEMAND REMAINS STRONG AMID SHIFTING MARKET CONDITIONS

Turner Construction Company announced that the First Quarter 2026 Turner Building Cost Index-which measures costs in the non-residential building construction market in the United States-increased to the value of 1530. This represents a 1.32% quarterly increase from the Fourth Quarter 2025 and a 4.87% yearly increase from the First Quarter 2025.

"Demand across the U.S. construction market remains solid, though conditions vary by region and sector. High-growth areas such as data centers, manufacturing, and semiconductor facilities are driving strong demand, particularly in the Midwest and Southeast, while traditional commercial construction has softened in several markets. At the same time, architecture and engineering activity appears to be stabilizing. Material costs such as steel, aluminum, and copper have experienced increases during the first quarter," said Attilio Rivetti, the Turner vice president responsible for compiling the Cost Index. "We are closely monitoring rising oil prices, which could begin to impact transportation and material costs in the coming quarter."

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION STRONGER MINDS, STRONGER TEAMS: EMPOWERING THE BIAL TEAM

Investing in people is the cornerstone of high-performance culture. We recently concluded an intensive training program for the Bengaluru International Airport team (BIAL), centered on the theme "Stronger Minds, Stronger Teams!!"

the session delved deep into performance excellence and the attitudes that drive successful outcomes. From mastering self-direction to understanding the emotional drivers behind decision-making, the day was packed with actionable insights designed to elevate both personal and professional growth.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. DAVID CAMPBELL NAMED TO AZ BIG MEDIA'S 2026 "PEOPLE TO KNOW IN COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE" LIST

PHOENIX, Arizona (November 6, 2025) - PCL Buildings Inc. is pleased to announce that David Campbell, district manager for PCL Construction's Phoenix buildings office, has been named to AZ Big Media's "2026 People and Projects to Know in Commercial Real Estate" list. This annual recognition celebrates influential leaders who are shaping the future of Arizona's commercial real estate industry.

"This recognition highlights the momentum we're building in Arizona's construction market and our commitment to contributing positively to the region's future," says Campbell. "I'm proud to represent PCL and support the growth and innovation happening across Arizona."

Campbell was named as the leader of PCL's newest commercial buildings office in Phoenix this past spring, building upon a nearly 40-year legacy of excellence in water and civil construction across the Southwest. The Phoenix Buildings office marks a strategic milestone in PCL's commitment to the region, offering construction expertise across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, aviation, and commercial and retail development.

His appearance on AZ Big Media's list reflects Campbell's contributions to the industry over the course of his nearly 20-year construction career, having led a wide range of high-profile projects across the U.S. Campbell joined PCL in 2005, with early contributions to entertainment and hospitality developments in Orlando. He later served as senior project manager on the American Dream megamall in New Jersey and held several leadership roles at PCL's U.S. head office in Denver before his current role in Phoenix.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

urn:contentItem:6JGF-SRG3-RTDN-M3YJ-00000-00WALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY - INDUSTRY PULSE: KEY TRENDS AND PEER INSIGHTS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:39ZLexisNexisSTOCKWALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY - INDUSTRY PULSE: KEY TRENDS AND PEER INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: WALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY

PermID5000066406Websitehttp://www.walbridge.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress777 Woodward Ave., Ste. 300 Detroit, MI 48226

ACTIVITIES:

Walbridge Aldinger Company, founded in 1916, is a construction management firm headquartered in Detroit, Michigan. With over 1,500 employees, it serves automotive, commercial, and industrial sectors, delivering projects like manufacturing plants and data centers. Generating over $2 billion annually, Walbridge is known for its lean construction practices and safety focus. In 2024, it expanded its data center and renewable energy project portfolio, capitalizing on tech infrastructure demand. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and employee development, with strong community engagement initiatives. Competing with Gilbane, Walbridge stands out for its automotive expertise and client-centric approach. Recent initiatives include investments in sustainable construction and digital project management tools, reinforcing its industry leadership.

+ WALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY [1 Press Release]

+ WALBRIDGE IMPROVES ECOVADIS SCORE, RANKING AMONG TOP-RATED COMPANIES IN 2025

Building on the foundation Walbridge has established, our sustainability journey continues to gain momentum. Our 2025 EcoVadis assessment score increased from 60 to 67, placing us in the 77th percentile among more than 150,000 companies rated worldwide. This year's results reflect meaningful progress across several key sustainability categories and reinforce our standing as one of the top-rated companies in our industry and among companies of similar size.

EcoVadis is a globally recognized platform for measuring sustainability performance across business operations. The assessment reviews 21 criteria across Environment, Labor and Human Rights, Ethics and Sustainable Procurement. We continue to leverage EcoVadis feedback to refine our sustainability strategies and strengthen areas for continued progress.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [4 Press Releases]

+ BARTON MALOW + SHERIDAN CONSTRUCTION SET TO BUILD NEW MACON ARENA

MACON, GEORGIA - Barton Malow, in association with Sheridan Construction, has been chosen as the Construction Manager for the new Macon Arena, a major project that will replace the current Macon Coliseum and serve as a key part of the region's expanding entertainment opportunities.

Located southeast of Atlanta in Macon-Bibb County, the planned facility is designed to host various multipurpose events like minor league hockey, basketball games, high school tournaments, concerts, conferences, trade shows, and more. The new arena will offer a top-tier guest experience, featuring premium seating options like luxury suites, club suites, and loge boxes, as well as modern fan amenities such as clubs, concessions, restrooms, locker rooms, and integrated technologies. Back-of-house and support spaces will ensure the venue functions as a state-of-the-art civic facility, offering performers, event planners, and organizations a great experience.

"This arena won't be just a venue, it will be a destination and major economic engine that creates opportunities, attracts visitors, and keeps our city competitive for years to come," said Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller.

As Macon's entertainment landscape continues to expand with the new Atrium Health Amphitheater and the recently renovated Macon City Auditorium, the new arena is poised to further elevate the region's growing lineup of elite venues.

"We're thrilled to partner with Sheridan Construction and bring Barton Malow's expertise to Macon-Bibb to deliver a top-tier arena. The new venue is set to become a central location in Georgia for sports, entertainment, and community gatherings," said Barton Malow Office Director Blake Boyd. "This project is an investment in Macon-Bibb County's future, and we're excited to support the local community every step of the way."

Adds Sheridan Construction President + CEO Christy Kovac. "It's a privilege for our firm to return to Macon's legacy of entertainment and civic pride, having built the original coliseum 57 years ago. Partnering with Barton Malow on this transformative new arena is both an honor and a responsibility. We believe this project will serve as a powerful economic and tourism engine, attracting visitors, creating opportunities, and enriching the lives of everyone in our community for generations to come."

Construction is planned to commence in summer 2026, with the project anticipated to reach completion in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY LUKE KNOCHEL PROMOTED TO VICE PRESIDENT OF PROJECT CONTROLS

Luke's project controls leadership role spans multiple geographic regions and consists of overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Barton Malow has announced the promotion of Luke Knochel to Vice President of Project Controls.

In his new role, Luke leads a multi‑disciplinary team responsible for core functions such as cost and schedule management, forecasting, project start‑up, accounts receivable and payable processes, and change management. His leadership spans multiple geographic regions, overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Luke started at Barton Malow in 2013 as a Cost Analyst and has served in various project controls and finance leadership roles throughout his tenure. He oversaw accounts payable and accounts receivable, change management, forecasting, reporting, and more on the Little Caesars Arena project, then went on to supervise Barton Malow's offices in Chicago, Columbus, and Nashville. After a promotion to Project Controls Manager overseeing the then-Midwest business unit, Luke transitioned into a corporate finance role managing the shared service department and implementing new accounts payable software.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY BUILDING FOR THE BRAND: CONSTRUCTING COLLEGE ATHLETIC FACILITIES IN THE NIL ERA

NIL athletic construction: Many modern facilities now include dedicated content creation zones where athletes can produce high-quality photos and videos for social media and sponsorship campaigns.

On July 1, 2021, the NCAA formally adopted a policy allowing college athletes to earn money for their name, image, and likeness (NIL).

At the time, one of my acquaintances, Matt Wilson, was serving as the Senior Director of Development for Athletics at Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University. He witnessed firsthand the immediate impact that NIL had on college athletics.

Today, Matt sees things from the other side of the table. After leaving Embry-Riddle in 2022, he spent two years as Executive Director of NIL Collective Operations + Development at Athlete Forward Initiative. Now he runs his own consulting group designed to provide leadership, fundraising, communication, and culture development training for college athletic departments.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW EARNS ECOVADIS BRONZE MEDAL FOR SUSTAINABILITY EXCELLENCE

Barton Malow has been awarded the EcoVadis Bronze Medal, placing the construction enterprise among the top 35% of companies worldwide for sustainability performance. This achievement underscores Barton Malow's leadership in green building practices, ethical business operations, and sustainable procurement.

"This recognition marks a significant milestone in our ongoing commitment to integrating sustainability principles across our business and reflects the progress we've made in partnership with our clients and stakeholders," said Senior Director of Sustainability Jill Katic.

EcoVadis, the world's most trusted provider of business sustainability ratings, evaluates companies on four key themes: Environment, Labor & Human Rights, Ethics, and Sustainable Procurement.

Barton Malow is committed to creating a positive impact on the planet, its people, and the communities in which it builds. To learn more about how Barton Malow is working towards its long-term environmental commitments, like reducing carbon emissions and implementing green building practices, explore the 2025 Sustainability Impact Report.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION U.S. CONSTRUCTION DEMAND REMAINS STRONG AMID SHIFTING MARKET CONDITIONS

Turner Construction Company announced that the First Quarter 2026 Turner Building Cost Index-which measures costs in the non-residential building construction market in the United States-increased to the value of 1530. This represents a 1.32% quarterly increase from the Fourth Quarter 2025 and a 4.87% yearly increase from the First Quarter 2025.

"Demand across the U.S. construction market remains solid, though conditions vary by region and sector. High-growth areas such as data centers, manufacturing, and semiconductor facilities are driving strong demand, particularly in the Midwest and Southeast, while traditional commercial construction has softened in several markets. At the same time, architecture and engineering activity appears to be stabilizing. Material costs such as steel, aluminum, and copper have experienced increases during the first quarter," said Attilio Rivetti, the Turner vice president responsible for compiling the Cost Index. "We are closely monitoring rising oil prices, which could begin to impact transportation and material costs in the coming quarter."

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. DAVID CAMPBELL NAMED TO AZ BIG MEDIA'S 2026 "PEOPLE TO KNOW IN COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE" LIST

PHOENIX, Arizona (November 6, 2025) - PCL Buildings Inc. is pleased to announce that David Campbell, district manager for PCL Construction's Phoenix buildings office, has been named to AZ Big Media's "2026 People and Projects to Know in Commercial Real Estate" list. This annual recognition celebrates influential leaders who are shaping the future of Arizona's commercial real estate industry.

"This recognition highlights the momentum we're building in Arizona's construction market and our commitment to contributing positively to the region's future," says Campbell. "I'm proud to represent PCL and support the growth and innovation happening across Arizona."

Campbell was named as the leader of PCL's newest commercial buildings office in Phoenix this past spring, building upon a nearly 40-year legacy of excellence in water and civil construction across the Southwest. The Phoenix Buildings office marks a strategic milestone in PCL's commitment to the region, offering construction expertise across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, aviation, and commercial and retail development.

His appearance on AZ Big Media's list reflects Campbell's contributions to the industry over the course of his nearly 20-year construction career, having led a wide range of high-profile projects across the U.S. Campbell joined PCL in 2005, with early contributions to entertainment and hospitality developments in Orlando. He later served as senior project manager on the American Dream megamall in New Jersey and held several leadership roles at PCL's U.S. head office in Denver before his current role in Phoenix.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JG9-SPG3-RSVN-10BW-00000-00How DTE Energy Has Fared Since Its Recent Analyst Ratings Changes *Updated*2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisHow DTE Energy Has Fared Since Its Recent Analyst Ratings Changes *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, is up 19% Year to Date. There have been changes in analyst ratings and target prices in 2026 - some forward-looking and others that have been after price movements. Below are the recent analyst ratings changes and the change in company stock price since.

Rating DateAnalyst FirmRatingTarget PriceReturn Since Change (%)21 Apr, 2026Morgan Stanleyto Overweight156 to 1555.726 Mar, 2026BMO Capital Marketsto Market Perform157 to 1485.117 Mar, 2026BarclaysEqual Weight151 to 1561.620 Feb, 2026Morgan StanleyOverweight1535.419 Feb, 2026BMO Capital MarketsMarket Perform1575.418 Feb, 2026MizuhoOutperform144 to 1557.218 Feb, 2026CitigroupBuy158 to 1687.202 Feb, 2026Morgan StanleyOverweight142 to 14315.028 Jan, 2026Jefferies Financial GroupBuy150 to 15312.026 Jan, 2026MizuhoOutperform140 to 14412.8

Company Profile

DTE Energy, headquartered in Detroit, operates as a diversified energy company with its primary focus on regulated electric and natural gas utilities. The company serves 2.3 million electric customers in Southeast Michigan and 1.4 million gas customers statewide, making these utilities its largest revenue sources. Its portfolio also includes non-utility businesses specializing in custom energy solutions, renewable energy generation, and energy marketing and trading. The fastest-growing segments include clean energy development, such as solar parks under the MIGreenPower program, and hyperscale data center contracts like the 1.4 GW Oracle agreement. These initiatives drive economic progress, affordability benefits, and advancements toward net-zero goals.

value.Title: DTE Energy 's dividend in perspective: comparison with Xcel Energy Inc. *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-3VX3-RV59-43B7-00000-00DTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Xcel Energy Inc. *Updated*2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisDTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Xcel Energy Inc. *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy's trailing twelve months dividend is $4.51. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.97%. Its current price is at a 1.1% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 21.1% premium to its 52 week low. If DTE Energy was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.6%.

In comparison, Xcel Energy Inc.'s trailing twelve months dividend is $2.30. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.78%. Its current price is at a 0.8% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 30.1% premium to its 52 week low. If Xcel Energy Inc. was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.61%.

$1000 invested into DTE Energy stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,679 today. This total gain of $679 is generated by a capital gain of $456 and dividends of $223.

$1000 invested into Xcel Energy Inc. stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,596 today. This total gain of $596 is generated by a capital gain of $378 and dividends of $218.

DTE Energy's dividend has grown 7.1% over the last 4 years.

Xcel Energy Inc.'s dividend has grown 5.1% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

DTE Energy, headquartered in Detroit, operates as a diversified energy company with its primary focus on regulated electric and natural gas utilities. The company serves 2.3 million electric customers in Southeast Michigan and 1.4 million gas customers statewide, making these utilities its largest revenue sources. Its portfolio also includes non-utility businesses specializing in custom energy solutions, renewable energy generation, and energy marketing and trading. The fastest-growing segments include clean energy development, such as solar parks under the MIGreenPower program, and hyperscale data center contracts like the 1.4 GW Oracle agreement. These initiatives drive economic progress, affordability benefits, and advancements toward net-zero goals.

Company Profile

Xcel Energy operates as a regulated utility, providing electricity and natural gas services across eight U.S. states. Electric revenues represent the largest source of income, contributing $12.16 billion, or 83% of total operating revenues in 2025. These revenues are primarily driven by residential, commercial, and industrial sales. Natural gas revenues, accounting for $2.45 billion or 17% of total revenues, serve heating and industrial needs. The fastest-growing segments include electric sales, which increased by 2% on a weather-normalized basis, fueled by demand from commercial and industrial sectors, particularly in the energy sector and emerging data center loads. The company's infrastructure investments in renewables and transmission further support its long-term growth strategy.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE $142.43), the NYSE's 7th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, has slipped $4.30 (or 2.9%) in the past week to close at $142.43. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 212.1 points (or 3.0%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -5.9%. The volume was 2 times average weekly trading of 6.8 million shares.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 4.7% was 1 time its average weekly volatility of 3.8%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $894.4 million to $29.6 billion.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 11.9% is better than sector average of 9.9%. This is computed as net profit margin of 9.2% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.29 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 4.4.

3.3 Oversold/Bullish Signals:

- The stock is oversold according to the Williams % R indicator of -84.6, suggesting the price is close to its 14-day low of $140.37.

- The Stochastic indicator of 6.1 has pierced the oversold line of 20; this indicates the price is close to its 14-day low and is likely to revert to an uptrend.

SECTION 4 ONGOING BULLISH PARAMETERS

4.1 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 27%, EBITDA margin is 21.4% and the sum of the two 48.4% needs to exceed 40%.

4.2 Created Market Value [CMV] past 18 yrs of $19.8 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $22.5 billion from $7.1 billion to $29.6 billion in the last 18 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2.7 billion and Created Market Value of $19.8 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $8.35.

4.3 Satisfies two criteria of Benjamin Graham

-""Stability of growth in earnings over the last 5 years, defined as no more than two declines of 5% or greater in year-end earnings""; there has been only 1 decline in earnings in the last 5 years.

- Positive net income.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in current ratio from 0.7 to 0.8.

- Total shares on issue unchanged.

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Improvement in asset turnover.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

NYSE END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE $140.60), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, has fell $8.19 (or 5.5%) in the past week to close at $140.60.

This makes it the thirty-first lowest performer in the S&P 500 Index which was up 2.3% in the past week; a relative price change of -7.8%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 5.8% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 3.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $1.7 billion to $29.2 billion.

This has been exacerbated by high volume of 1.9 times average for the week.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

Financial News

Quarterly Report (Q1/2026):

Release Date: April 30, 2026

Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: DTE Energy sequential quarterly profits hit by 64% fall

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE), announced net profit of $247m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 64% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 44% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 44.4%, Revenue was up 15.8% and Net Profit was down 44.5%.

Friday's price drop

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) in 6th consecutive fall

DETROIT, Friday, May 08 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $140.57) stock price slipped further on Friday, sliding an additional $1.43 (1.0%) to close at $140.57. This brought its 6-day fall to 7.3%; compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 187.6 points (2.6%) in the six days for a relative price change of -9.9%. The stock decreased 0.7% on Wednesday and 0.3% on Thursday. The fall was exacerbated by a cumulative 6-day volume of 1.8 times the average 6-day volume of 1.4 million shares. There were 1,279,125 shares worth $179.8 million traded today.

1.4 STOCK SNIPPETS - ESG

July 26 2023: DTE Energy Michigan Public Service Commission approves DTEs landmark clean energy plan

Plan expedites end of coal while building enough Michigan-made renewables to power ~4 million homes

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) today received approval from the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) on the Companys landmark CleanVision Integrated Resource Plan (IRP). This approval comes just 14 days after a historic settlement agreement was reached between DTE and nearly two dozen organizations on the Companys 20-year plan that dramatically transforms how DTE generates electricity as part of its mission to invest in the future of Michigan. Developed over the past two years with the input of DTEs customers and other key stakeholders from across Michigan, this proposal accelerates DTEs clean energy transition increasing investments in Michigan-made solar and wind energy, speeding up the retirement of coal plants, and developing new energy storage all reinforcing DTEs commitment to cleaner energy generated reliably and affordably.

We want to thank the Michigan Public Service Commission for approving our CleanVision Plan. This has been a collaborative process, with input from key stakeholders, to ensure we have the best plan possible for our 2.3 million customers and the state of Michigan, said Jerry Norcia, chairman and chief executive officer, DTE Energy. We are transforming how we generate electricity with the goal of getting as clean as we can as fast as we can while ensuring we continue to produce energy that is reliable and affordable.

DTE Electric will surpass its previously announced carbon emission reduction goals targeting 65% in five years (2028), 85% in nine years (2032), 90% by 2040 and net zero carbon emissions by 2050.

Key details of the CleanVision Integrated Resource Plan include:

Investing in clean and reliable energy by:

Delivering long-term customer value by:

A broad range of stakeholders from across Michigan signed onto a settlement agreement that was submitted to the MPSC for approval earlier this month.

July 12 2023: DTE Energy and Michigan stakeholders reach historic clean energy settlement agreement

Transformational plan projects $2.5 billion in reduced future costs to customers1 while building enough Michigan-made renewables to power ~4 million homes

DETROIT, July 12, 2023 DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) today announced a historic settlement agreement with nearly two dozen organizations from across Michigan on the Companys transformational CleanVision Integrated Resource Plan. This 20-year plan dramatically transforms how the Company generates electricity as part of its mission to invest in the future of Michigan, with an energy proposal that adapts to how the Companys customers live and work today while meeting the energy demands of tomorrow. Developed over the past two years with the input of DTEs customers and stakeholders, this proposal continues DTEs clean energy transition by accelerating investments in Michigan-made solar and wind energy, speeding up the retirement of coal plants, and developing new energy storage all reinforcing DTEs commitment to cleaner energy generated reliably and affordably.

Our CleanVision Integrated Resource Plan will end our use of coal in 2032 while developing enough Michigan-made renewables to power approximately 4 million homes, said Jerry Norcia, chairman and chief executive officer, DTE Energy. Todays historic agreement is an investment in Michigans future, and we are grateful that 21 organizations from across Michigan have joined us in bringing our proposal one step closer to reality. This partnership and dedication have helped us build the best plan possible for our customers. We are also proud this plan puts our customers first by reducing the future costs of our clean energy transformation by $2.5 billion, while reliably generating cleaner, affordable energy now, and for generations to come.

DTE Electric will surpass its previously announced carbon emission reduction goals targeting 85% in nine years (2032), 90% by 2040 and net zero carbon emissions by 2050.

December 08 2022: DTE Energy retires two coal plants as part of its vision to generate carbon-free power for the grid of the future

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE), Michigans largest producer of clean energy, today announced the retirement of two coal-fired power plants, bringing Southeast Michigan closer to a carbon-free future with more affordable and reliable energy. DTE is also retraining displaced workers for jobs elsewhere in the company.

The retirement of the Trenton Channel and St. Clair coal power plants marks a significant milestone on DTEs transformational plans to reach net zero, with nearly one-third of all electricity generated by the electric company now coming from carbon-free resources, such as wind, solar and nuclear energy. This past summer, DTE began the long-planned retirement of both plants, placing the St. Clair Power facility on standby-only status to meet periods of high customer demand or extreme temperatures. With the year drawing to a close, DTE has officially retired both plants, and employees at both locations were offered opportunities to continue to work for the enterprise.

Retiring these plants is another step in DTEs plan to deliver affordable, reliable and clean energy through a modern grid that meets the needs of our customers changing lifestyles and our states new economy, said Jerry Norcia, chairman and chief executive officer, DTE Energy. These steps today will lead to a cleaner environment and energy future for Michigan families, communities and businesses.

For nearly 100 years, the St. Clair and Trenton Channel power plants proudly served southeast Michigan residents. Built to address the growth of Michigans post-World War I & II economy, the Trenton Channel Power Plant (operated from 1924 2022) and St. Clair Power Plant (operated from 1953 2022) were witnesses to history.

At the time of its commissioning, the Trenton Channel plant was the fourth major power plant Detroit Edison (now DTE Energy) put into operation and the largest project the company had undertaken.

November 23 2022: DTE Energy annual sustainability report highlights company efforts toward a clean energy future

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) today released its 2021 Sustainability Report, the company's annual publication detailing its environmental, social and governance (ESG) efforts.

The report, entitled "DTEs CleanVision" illustrates DTEs progress toward creating a clean energy future and provides key performance information in priority areas ranging from reliability upgrades and miles of trees trimmed, to net zero commitments and workforce development programs.

"At DTE, we strive to do whats right for our employees, customers, communities and stakeholders, not necessarily whats easy. That means ESG matters guide us in how we do business, including in our operations, environmental efforts, corporate governance, corporate citizenship and human capital management, said Jerry Norcia, DTE Chairman and CEO. This report provides the opportunity to share our progress through performance as we pursue our commitment to be best in the world and best for the world.

DTEs Sustainability Report highlights the companys work on key topics in ESG that are important to customers, employees, investors and other community stakeholders, while also guiding readers to where they can access more detailed information about specific topics of interest. This years report is supported by standard frameworks that guide its disclosures, including the Edison Electric Institute (EEI) and American Gas Association (AGA) ESG/Sustainability templates, the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI), the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) and Sustainable Accounting Standards Board (SASB).

Find the full report here

November 03 2022: DTE Energy proposes transformational investment in Michigan-made renewable energy and accelerates emission reductions

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) today released its CleanVision plan, a 20-year proposal to dramatically transform how the company generates electricity as part of its clean energy journey, all while investing in a modern grid that is reliable and affordable. The proposed plan increases investment in solar and wind energy, accelerates the retirement of coal plants, and includes the development of new energy storage all reinforcing DTEs commitment to cleaner energy.

Across the country, and here in Michigan, the energy landscape is changing rapidly and fundamentally, said Jerry Norcia, chairman and chief executive officer, DTE Energy. We are excited to drive this transformation by powering the grid with Michigan-made renewable energy, existing nuclear and natural gas. These new solar and wind assets, enough to power approximately 4 million households, will replace our retiring coal power plants, resulting in a cleaner environment for Michigan families, communities and businesses.

We are proud our plan invests $9 billion into Michigans economy over the next 10 years, supporting more than 25,000 Michigan jobs and the great, local communities we partner with across the state. This transformational plan is complimented by our announcement last year to invest an additional $8 billion to modernize the electric grid, making it more resilient to extreme weather and more reliable as we plan for increased customer demand.

DTEs proposal will be filed with the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) in what is referred to as an Integrated Resource Plan (IRP). Key details of DTEs proposal include:

By investing in cleaner sources of energy and accelerating the retirement of coal, DTE Electric plans to surpass its previously announced carbon emission reduction goals. The Companys emission reduction goals are ahead of the timelines in the MI Healthy Climate Plan (MHCP) and will help support Michigans economy-wide greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions reductions interim timelines.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

value.Title: Big Oil: Michigan antitrust lawsuit is purely political

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-VJ03-SFP5-52PB-00000-00Big Oil: Michigan antitrust lawsuit is purely political2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:04ZLexisNexisNEWSLETTERBig Oil: Michigan antitrust lawsuit is purely politicalJohn O'Brien

LANSING, Mich. – The oil industry is attacking Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel's attempt to use antitrust laws in a federal lawsuit alleging companies conspired to hold down competition from renewable-energy products.

Nessel sued companies like Exxon, Chevon and BP in January, alleging a "cartel" to maintain dominance in the transportation and primary energy markets that left consumers paying high prices. The companies responded earlier this month in a motion to dismiss that said her claims require more than speculation.

"This is not an antitrust case," the motion says. "It is an attempt by Michigan to use the antitrust laws to advance its current policy goals of promoting renewable energy over fossil fuels.

"To state a claim, Michigan must plausibly plead standing, an actual agreement among Defendants that unreasonably restrains competition in a properly defined relevant market, and actionable conduct within the statute of limitations. The complaint pleas none of those things."

While the world waits for the U.S. Supreme Court to decide whether government officials can sue Big Oil for state-law claims like public nuisance and violations of consumer protection laws, Nessel embraced filing in federal court under the Sherman Antitrust Act, the Clayton Antitrust Act and the Michigan Antitrust Reform Act.

Her lawsuit alleged the companies abandoned renewable energy products, used patent manipulation and litigation to hinder competitors and suppressed information about the costs of fossil fuels and the viability of alternatives.

Her lawsuit said electric vehicles would be "rolling off assembly lines in Flint" had it not been for the oil industry. Solar, wind and other energies would power homes and businesses, she said. Fossil fuels would take a backseat if not for the conspiracy, the suit says.

"Instead, Michigan consumers today remain locked in transportation and primary energy markets that have failed to evolve – not because clean alternatives are not viable, but because Defendants have suppressed the conditions for their otherwise-inevitable deployment and adoption," the complaint says.

The companies say these are points for a political debate – not an antitrust lawsuit – and Nessel can't show that if they invested more in renewable energy, innovation would have followed.

The U.S. Supreme Court is waiting on briefs in a lawsuit brought by Boulder and will decide if the Colorado Supreme Court was right to let it proceed. Hawaii's top court reached a similar ruling.

But Maryland's did not, affirming dismissals of cases in Baltimore and Annapolis. State judges in Pennsylvania, South Carolina, New York, Delaware and New Jersey also threw cases out as improper attempts to regulate emissions.

Alabama Attorney General Steve Marshall led a group of 26 states that said Boulder is trying to "assert a power with no analogue in our Nation's history and no place in our federalism."

Republicans in Congress also filed a brief, complaining the Colorado Supreme Court's ruling created a patchwork of state and local regulations for a market that is supposed to be overseen by federal authorities.

And the Trump administration, which has issued an executive order forbidding any new cases and is battling Nessel in court, submitted its thoughts last year, saying Boulder is attempting to impose new rules that would go far beyond the borders of Colorado.

"If, as the Colorado Supreme Court held, those theories are consistent with federal law, then every locality in the country could sue essentially anyone in the world for contributing to global climate change," that brief says

value.Title: Renewable energy news: Elisa Industriq, Rezolv , Enery , ESA

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-8H63-RTHK-N06F-00000-00Renewable energy news: Elisa Industriq, Rezolv, Enery, ESA2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisRenewable energy news: Elisa Industriq, Rezolv, Enery, ESANew Delhi


May 08 --

Today’s renewable energy news includes updates from Elisa Industriq, Rezolv, Enery, ESA, among others.

Elisa Industriq’s Gridle to Optimise 10MWh Akkux Battery System in Finland

Elisa Industriq has signed an agreement through its Gridle platform to optimise a 10MWh battery energy storage system operated by Akkux in Finland. The project marks another step in expanding AI-driven battery optimisation services across the Nordic energy market.  The Gridle platform uses artificial intelligence to manage battery operations, enabling participation in electricity reserve and balancing markets while improving efficiency and revenue generation. The optimisation service is designed to enhance flexibility, support renewable integration, and strengthen grid stability during periods of fluctuating power demand.  The collaboration highlights growing investment and innovation in smart energy management technologies, supporting Finland’s transition toward a more flexible and low-carbon electricity system while delivering long-term operational and sustainability benefits.



Rezolv Commissions 225MW Solar and Storage Project in Bulgaria

Rezolv Energy has inaugurated the 225MW St. George solar park in Silistra, Bulgaria, marking one of the country’s largest renewable energy projects. Built on the former Silistra airport site, the $300 million development includes nearly 400,000 solar panels capable of generating around 313GWh of electricity annually.  The project also features a 90MW/240MWh battery energy storage system supported by the European Union’s NextGenerationEU programme, improving grid flexibility and renewable integration. Around one-third of the solar output will be supplied to Ardagh Glass Packaging-Europe under a 12-year virtual power purchase agreement. Financing included €90 million from International Finance Corporation and Raiffeisen Bank International.  The project highlights major investment and innovation in hybrid solar-storage infrastructure, strengthening Bulgaria’s energy security, decarbonisation efforts, and long-term clean energy growth.

Enery Commissions 150MW Battery Storage Project in Bulgaria

Enery has commissioned the 150MW/601.8MWh Nova Zagora battery energy storage system in southeastern Bulgaria, now considered the largest operational battery project in Central and Eastern Europe. The standalone facility is designed to strengthen grid stability by storing excess electricity and supplying power during periods of peak demand.  The project includes 120 battery containers connected through a dedicated 33/110-kV substation and operates under a virtual power purchase agreement with energy trader Vitol. Financing was supported by a green loan package from DSK Bank, part of OTP Group.  The development highlights major investment and innovation in large-scale battery infrastructure, supporting renewable integration, reducing fossil fuel dependence, improving energy security, and accelerating Bulgaria’s transition toward a more flexible and resilient power system.

ESA Sells 150MW Michigan Battery Storage Project to Ørsted

ESA Solar Energy has sold the 150MW Salzburg battery energy storage project in Michigan to Ørsted, expanding Orsted’s growing US energy storage portfolio. The standalone project is strategically positioned to support Michigan’s clean energy and grid reliability goals, with commercial operations targeted between 2029 and 2030.  The Salzburg project includes a 150MW/600MWh battery system designed for peak shaving, load shifting and grid regulation services. Located in Midland Township, the facility will connect directly to Michigan’s transmission network through a dedicated substation.  The transaction highlights continued investment and innovation in large-scale battery storage infrastructure as utilities accelerate renewable integration. The project is expected to strengthen grid resilience, support Michigan’s transition toward 100 percent clean energy by 2040, and enhance long-term energy security across the region.

NYSE END-OF-MONTH STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE) stock price has now climbed $14.02 (or 11%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $142.0.

Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 7.2% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 3.8% for DTE Energy Company shareholders.

DTE Energy Company is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.
