SECTION 9 CORPORATE PROFILE

9.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

NYSE END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE $140.60), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, has fell $8.19 (or 5.5%) in the past week to close at $140.60.

This makes it the thirty-first lowest performer in the S&P 500 Index which was up 2.3% in the past week; a relative price change of -7.8%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 5.8% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 3.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $1.7 billion to $29.2 billion.

This has been exacerbated by high volume of 1.9 times average for the week.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

Friday's price drop

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) in 6th consecutive fall

DETROIT, Friday, May 08 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $140.57) stock price slipped further on Friday, sliding an additional $1.43 (1.0%) to close at $140.57. This brought its 6-day fall to 7.3%; compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 187.6 points (2.6%) in the six days for a relative price change of -9.9%. The stock decreased 0.7% on Wednesday and 0.3% on Thursday. The fall was exacerbated by a cumulative 6-day volume of 1.8 times the average 6-day volume of 1.4 million shares. There were 1,279,125 shares worth $179.8 million traded today.

1.4 STOCK SNIPPETS - ESG

July 26 2023: DTE Energy Michigan Public Service Commission approves DTEs landmark clean energy plan

Plan expedites end of coal while building enough Michigan-made renewables to power ~4 million homes

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) today received approval from the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) on the Companys landmark CleanVision Integrated Resource Plan (IRP). This approval comes just 14 days after a historic settlement agreement was reached between DTE and nearly two dozen organizations on the Companys 20-year plan that dramatically transforms how DTE generates electricity as part of its mission to invest in the future of Michigan. Developed over the past two years with the input of DTEs customers and other key stakeholders from across Michigan, this proposal accelerates DTEs clean energy transition increasing investments in Michigan-made solar and wind energy, speeding up the retirement of coal plants, and developing new energy storage all reinforcing DTEs commitment to cleaner energy generated reliably and affordably.

We want to thank the Michigan Public Service Commission for approving our CleanVision Plan. This has been a collaborative process, with input from key stakeholders, to ensure we have the best plan possible for our 2.3 million customers and the state of Michigan, said Jerry Norcia, chairman and chief executive officer, DTE Energy. We are transforming how we generate electricity with the goal of getting as clean as we can as fast as we can while ensuring we continue to produce energy that is reliable and affordable.

DTE Electric will surpass its previously announced carbon emission reduction goals targeting 65% in five years (2028), 85% in nine years (2032), 90% by 2040 and net zero carbon emissions by 2050.

Key details of the CleanVision Integrated Resource Plan include:

Investing in clean and reliable energy by:

Delivering long-term customer value by:

A broad range of stakeholders from across Michigan signed onto a settlement agreement that was submitted to the MPSC for approval earlier this month.

July 12 2023: DTE Energy and Michigan stakeholders reach historic clean energy settlement agreement

Transformational plan projects $2.5 billion in reduced future costs to customers1 while building enough Michigan-made renewables to power ~4 million homes

DETROIT, July 12, 2023 DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) today announced a historic settlement agreement with nearly two dozen organizations from across Michigan on the Companys transformational CleanVision Integrated Resource Plan. This 20-year plan dramatically transforms how the Company generates electricity as part of its mission to invest in the future of Michigan, with an energy proposal that adapts to how the Companys customers live and work today while meeting the energy demands of tomorrow. Developed over the past two years with the input of DTEs customers and stakeholders, this proposal continues DTEs clean energy transition by accelerating investments in Michigan-made solar and wind energy, speeding up the retirement of coal plants, and developing new energy storage all reinforcing DTEs commitment to cleaner energy generated reliably and affordably.

Our CleanVision Integrated Resource Plan will end our use of coal in 2032 while developing enough Michigan-made renewables to power approximately 4 million homes, said Jerry Norcia, chairman and chief executive officer, DTE Energy. Todays historic agreement is an investment in Michigans future, and we are grateful that 21 organizations from across Michigan have joined us in bringing our proposal one step closer to reality. This partnership and dedication have helped us build the best plan possible for our customers. We are also proud this plan puts our customers first by reducing the future costs of our clean energy transformation by $2.5 billion, while reliably generating cleaner, affordable energy now, and for generations to come.

DTE Electric will surpass its previously announced carbon emission reduction goals targeting 85% in nine years (2032), 90% by 2040 and net zero carbon emissions by 2050.

December 08 2022: DTE Energy retires two coal plants as part of its vision to generate carbon-free power for the grid of the future

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE), Michigans largest producer of clean energy, today announced the retirement of two coal-fired power plants, bringing Southeast Michigan closer to a carbon-free future with more affordable and reliable energy. DTE is also retraining displaced workers for jobs elsewhere in the company.

The retirement of the Trenton Channel and St. Clair coal power plants marks a significant milestone on DTEs transformational plans to reach net zero, with nearly one-third of all electricity generated by the electric company now coming from carbon-free resources, such as wind, solar and nuclear energy. This past summer, DTE began the long-planned retirement of both plants, placing the St. Clair Power facility on standby-only status to meet periods of high customer demand or extreme temperatures. With the year drawing to a close, DTE has officially retired both plants, and employees at both locations were offered opportunities to continue to work for the enterprise.

Retiring these plants is another step in DTEs plan to deliver affordable, reliable and clean energy through a modern grid that meets the needs of our customers changing lifestyles and our states new economy, said Jerry Norcia, chairman and chief executive officer, DTE Energy. These steps today will lead to a cleaner environment and energy future for Michigan families, communities and businesses.

For nearly 100 years, the St. Clair and Trenton Channel power plants proudly served southeast Michigan residents. Built to address the growth of Michigans post-World War I & II economy, the Trenton Channel Power Plant (operated from 1924 2022) and St. Clair Power Plant (operated from 1953 2022) were witnesses to history.

At the time of its commissioning, the Trenton Channel plant was the fourth major power plant Detroit Edison (now DTE Energy) put into operation and the largest project the company had undertaken.

November 23 2022: DTE Energy annual sustainability report highlights company efforts toward a clean energy future

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) today released its 2021 Sustainability Report, the company's annual publication detailing its environmental, social and governance (ESG) efforts.

The report, entitled "DTEs CleanVision" illustrates DTEs progress toward creating a clean energy future and provides key performance information in priority areas ranging from reliability upgrades and miles of trees trimmed, to net zero commitments and workforce development programs.

"At DTE, we strive to do whats right for our employees, customers, communities and stakeholders, not necessarily whats easy. That means ESG matters guide us in how we do business, including in our operations, environmental efforts, corporate governance, corporate citizenship and human capital management, said Jerry Norcia, DTE Chairman and CEO. This report provides the opportunity to share our progress through performance as we pursue our commitment to be best in the world and best for the world.

DTEs Sustainability Report highlights the companys work on key topics in ESG that are important to customers, employees, investors and other community stakeholders, while also guiding readers to where they can access more detailed information about specific topics of interest. This years report is supported by standard frameworks that guide its disclosures, including the Edison Electric Institute (EEI) and American Gas Association (AGA) ESG/Sustainability templates, the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI), the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) and Sustainable Accounting Standards Board (SASB).

Find the full report here

November 03 2022: DTE Energy proposes transformational investment in Michigan-made renewable energy and accelerates emission reductions

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) today released its CleanVision plan, a 20-year proposal to dramatically transform how the company generates electricity as part of its clean energy journey, all while investing in a modern grid that is reliable and affordable. The proposed plan increases investment in solar and wind energy, accelerates the retirement of coal plants, and includes the development of new energy storage all reinforcing DTEs commitment to cleaner energy.

Across the country, and here in Michigan, the energy landscape is changing rapidly and fundamentally, said Jerry Norcia, chairman and chief executive officer, DTE Energy. We are excited to drive this transformation by powering the grid with Michigan-made renewable energy, existing nuclear and natural gas. These new solar and wind assets, enough to power approximately 4 million households, will replace our retiring coal power plants, resulting in a cleaner environment for Michigan families, communities and businesses.

We are proud our plan invests $9 billion into Michigans economy over the next 10 years, supporting more than 25,000 Michigan jobs and the great, local communities we partner with across the state. This transformational plan is complimented by our announcement last year to invest an additional $8 billion to modernize the electric grid, making it more resilient to extreme weather and more reliable as we plan for increased customer demand.

DTEs proposal will be filed with the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) in what is referred to as an Integrated Resource Plan (IRP). Key details of DTEs proposal include:

By investing in cleaner sources of energy and accelerating the retirement of coal, DTE Electric plans to surpass its previously announced carbon emission reduction goals. The Companys emission reduction goals are ahead of the timelines in the MI Healthy Climate Plan (MHCP) and will help support Michigans economy-wide greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions reductions interim timelines.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. DETROIT, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $142.41) stock price extended its slide on Wednesday, shedding an additional $1.08 (0.8%) to close at $142.41.

The shares have tumbled $9.28 (6.1%) over the past four trading days; compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 152.7 points (2.1%) in the four days for a relative price change of -8.2%.

The fall was exacerbated by a cumulative 4-day volume of 1.8 times the average 4-day volume of 1.4 million shares. There were 1,546,286 shares worth $220.2 million traded today.

In the past five days trading has been on consistently high volumes; Volume Index (VI) has been 1.1 or higher.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS & PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 394 words

Fri May 1, 2026: DTE Energy Unveils 8.4 GW Data Center Growth Potential

DTE Energy Company is poised to capitalize on significant growth in the data center sector, with plans to support a 1.4-GW Oracle data center currently under construction. On the 1st of May, 2026, company officials revealed that they are also in discussions regarding an additional 7 GW of potential data center projects, indicating a robust pipeline that could reshape the utility's operations in the coming years.

During the company's first quarter earnings call, President and CEO Joi Harris noted that DTE Energy is making steady progress in finalizing contractual agreements to support this data center growth. The utility reported operating earnings of $407 million for Q1 2026, a slight decrease from $436 million in the same period of 2025, attributed to fluctuations in energy trading, tax timing, and elevated interest expenses. However, its electric subsidiary, DTE Electric, experienced an increase in operating earnings to $218 million, up from $147 million year-over-year, driven by favorable weather conditions and successful rate implementations.

The Michigan Public Service Commission granted conditional approval for the Oracle contract in December, paving the way for construction and phased load ramp-up over the next several years. Oracle's investment includes covering the full cost of its energy and capacity needs, supported by existing capabilities and planned energy storage. In addition, DTE Energy is awaiting approval for a 1-GW Google data center project, which is expected to come online by the end of 2028, bolstered by a mix of renewable generation and energy storage solutions.

Meeting the energy demands of Google could drive an estimated $5 billion in incremental investments in generation and storage through 2032. Harris emphasized that these investments are structured to protect existing customers while providing affordability benefits. The proposed 20-year power supply agreement with Google includes provisions designed to ensure that current customers are not adversely affected by the utility's expansion efforts.

Beyond the Oracle and Google projects, DTE Energy is in advanced discussions concerning additional data center projects that could add 2 GW of incremental load, with even more potential projects identified in the pipeline. As the utility continues to enhance its electric reliability—evidenced by a recent audit and a commitment to modernize its distribution system—DTE Energy remains confident in its ability to meet its long-term operating earnings per share growth target of 6-8% through 2030, supported by ongoing investments and strategic partnerships in the evolving data center landscape.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 359 words

Tue Apr 28, 2026: DTE Energy's Rate Freeze Plan Tied to Oracle's Data Centre Development

US utility DTE Energy has announced a plan to pause customer rate increases for two years, contingent upon the successful development of a US$16 billion data centre campus being constructed for tech giant Oracle. This announcement comes as DTE Energy prepares to file for regulatory approval with the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) on 28th April 2026, aiming for a rate freeze that extends through at least 2028.

Joi Harris, president and CEO of DTE Energy, emphasized the importance of the data centre project, stating, 'As long as the first data centre project we're supporting comes online as planned by the end of 2027 and we're able to receive other regulatory approvals, we will refrain from filing another rate request until at least 2028—providing customers two years without an increase in rates after the current request is complete.'

The two data centre contracts associated with the Oracle project are projected to contribute approximately US$9 billion to enhancements in DTE's electric system by 2045, thereby alleviating cost pressures on other customers. The utility's upcoming request for US$474.3 million will support significant investments in the electric grid and power generation, including the conversion of the Belle River Power Plant from coal to natural gas and the establishment of the 220MW/880MWh Trenton Channel Energy Center, which is expected to become the largest stand-alone battery energy storage facility in the Great Lakes region upon completion.

DTE Energy's construction of the battery energy storage system (BESS) commenced in 2024, following MPSC approval for the Trenton project. The utility had originally partnered with Powin for the BESS technology but shifted to LG Energy Solution after experiencing delays and a contract termination with Powin in June 2025. This strategic pivot is aimed at achieving a cost reduction of over US$30 million for the project.

The financing for Related Digital's data centre, which will utilize DTE Energy's power, involves equity from Related Digital and funds associated with Blackstone, alongside long-term fixed-rate debt anchored by Pacific Investment Management Company (PIMCO). The project is anticipated to generate significant economic benefits, including job creation and tax revenue for local communities, while also reinforcing DTE Energy's commitment to renewable energy solutions.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 298 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: City Opera House Partners with DTE Energy for Sustainable Initiatives

TRAVERSE CITY — The City Opera House has named DTE Energy as its first green sponsor to support environmentally conscious operations. As of 24th April 2026, this partnership marks a significant step towards implementing sustainability initiatives within the organization.

The collaboration aims to enhance eco-friendly practices, including the transition to rechargeable systems for microphone packs, which will eliminate the need for disposable batteries. Additionally, the City Opera House is adopting recycled copy paper throughout its operations, encompassing all printed program inserts. Future plans involve installing a bike rack to promote alternative transportation options for both patrons and staff.

Chad Lindsey, the executive director of the City Opera House, emphasized the importance of this partnership, stating, “DTE Energy's partnership helps us make thoughtful, earth-conscious decisions that align with our values as a historic community anchor. Together, we're ensuring that our operations reflect not only artistic excellence but environmental responsibility.” This sentiment underscores the venue's commitment to integrating sustainability into its core mission.

Henry Decker, vice president of gas sales and supply at DTE Energy, reiterated the company's dedication to sustainability, remarking, “We look forward to supporting the City Opera House as they continue to make a meaningful difference for the environment and for the people they serve.” DTE Energy is known for its initiatives that help customers reduce their carbon footprints, such as the CleanVision Natural Gas Balance program, which balances carbon emissions from natural gas use by supporting Michigan forest management and the development of renewable natural gas.

The City Opera House serves as a cultural hub for NorthernMichigan and remains committed to reducing its environmental footprint. The collaboration with DTE Energy is a promising step towards fostering a more sustainable future, reflecting a growing trend among cultural institutions to prioritize environmental responsibility alongside their artistic missions.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 72 words

Operational Veteran Joi Harris Elected CEO to Navigate Decarbonization and Grid Challenges

Joi Harris, formerly President and COO, has been appointed CEO and President of DTE Energy, succeeding Jerry Norcia. Her extensive operational experience is expected to guide the company through its decarbonization strategy and grid modernization challenges. While her appointment is viewed as a move for continuity, she faces significant regulatory scrutiny over rates and service quality, and her performance will be measured by her ability to meet growth targets amidst these pressures.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 70 words

DTE Energy Outperforms Q3 Expectations Despite Revenue Headwinds, Reaffirms Guidance

DTE Energy reported Q3 2025 operating earnings of $2.25 per share, surpassing estimates despite a nearly $1 billion revenue shortfall caused by lower fuel cost pass-throughs. The earnings beat was driven by cost management and tax credit timing in the DTE Electric segment. Management reaffirmed its full-year guidance, signaling financial stability, but also noted that future growth will depend more on industrial and data center demand rather than residential load.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

MPSC Rejection of Saline Data Center Contract Signals Regulatory Headwinds for Tech Load Growth

DTE Energy's plan to build a major data center in Saline Township was blocked after the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) failed to approve a special contract before a key deadline. The project was central to DTE's strategy of offsetting stagnant residential demand. The rejection, based on concerns about cost-shifting to residential customers and grid stability, sets a challenging precedent for future large-scale industrial projects and introduces significant execution risk to DTE's growth thesis.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 63 words

Regulator Authorizes $217 Million Rate Increase, Capping Revenue Below Utility Request

The MPSC approved a $217.4 million rate increase for DTE Electric, which was only 48% of the company's original request. While the revenue supports grid modernization and near-term financials, the significant reduction signals a tightening regulatory environment. This outcome forces DTE to file for rate increases more frequently to recover its infrastructure investments, a strategy that risks creating friction with regulators and consumers.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 67 words

DTE Files $574 Million Rate Case Amidst Heightened Political Scrutiny

Shortly after its last rate adjustment, DTE Energy filed for another increase, requesting $574.1 million to fund a $4.4 billion investment in grid hardening and cleaner energy. The request has drawn immediate political opposition, notably from the Michigan Attorney General. The outcome is critical for DTE's ability to earn its authorized return and execute its reliability improvement strategy, but a significant disallowance could threaten its growth targets.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 64 words

Investment Plan Expanded by $6.5 Billion to Support Grid Modernization and Load Growth

DTE Energy has increased its five-year capital investment plan by $6.5 billion to accelerate grid modernization and expand renewable generation. The expansion is intended to support a 6-8% annual EPS growth rate, driven by projected load increases from electrification and data centers. However, this large-scale plan faces significant execution risk, including the need for regulatory approval for cost recovery and potential supply chain constraints.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 58 words

Commissioning of Polaris Solar Park Advances Voluntary Green Power Strategy

DTE Energy has brought the 100-megawatt Polaris Solar Park into commercial operation. As a key part of its MIGreenPower program, the facility allows corporate customers to meet their ESG goals. The project's success demonstrates DTE's ability to execute utility-scale solar developments, although the scalability of this model faces future challenges related to land availability and local community opposition.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 63 words

Construction Begins on Dedicated Solar Array for Ford Motor Company

DTE has started construction on a utility-scale solar park dedicated to supplying power to Ford Motor Company's EV manufacturing operations. This project secures long-term revenue from a major industrial partner and mitigates the risk of load defection. While it reinforces DTE's "energy-as-a-service" model, it also ties a portion of the utility's performance to the cyclical automotive industry and Ford's specific EV strategy success.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 65 words

Conversion of Belle River Plant Commences, Pivoting Coal Assets to Peaker Role

DTE has begun converting a coal-fired unit at its Belle River Power Plant to a natural gas peaker plant. The $154 million project is a strategic step to ensure grid reliability as the company retires coal plants and integrates more renewables. This conversion repurposes an existing asset to maintain resource adequacy cost-effectively, though the long-term viability of gas assets remains subject to future environmental policy.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 68 words

Advanced Negotiations for 3 GW Data Center Load Define Long-Term Growth Thesis

DTE Energy is in advanced negotiations to supply approximately 3 GW of power to prospective data center clients, a volume that could transform its commercial load profile. This potential demand is central to the company's long-term growth strategy. However, realizing this pipeline is contingent on overcoming significant regulatory hurdles related to cost allocation and ensuring sufficient generation capacity to serve such a large load without compromising grid reliability.

SECTION 4 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 05 2026: DTE Energy (DTE) Shows Strong Growth Potential Amid Market Trends

DTE Energy Company, a diversified energy provider based in Detroit, MI, is emerging as a strong growth stock for investors seeking reliable returns. As of 6th May 2026, DTE Energy holds a #2 (Buy) rating on the Zacks Rank, indicating robust potential for capital appreciation in the coming months. This rating is bolstered by a favorable VGM Score of B, which aggregates value, growth, and momentum indicators, making DTE Energy an attractive option for both growth and value investors.

DTE Energy's growth prospects are underscored by its Growth Style Score of B, suggesting an anticipated year-over-year earnings growth of 6% for the current fiscal year. This growth trajectory is supported by recent upward revisions from four analysts regarding the company's earnings estimates over the last 60 days. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for fiscal 2025 has risen by $0.02, bringing it to $7.24 per share. May 01 2026: DTE Energy Unveils 8.4 GW Data Center Growth Potential

DTE Energy Company is poised to capitalize on significant growth in the data center sector, with plans to support a 1.4-GW Oracle data center currently under construction. On the 1st of May, 2026, company officials revealed that they are also in discussions regarding an additional 7 GW of potential data center projects, indicating a robust pipeline that could reshape the utility's operations in the coming years.

During the company’s first quarter earnings call, President and CEO Joi Harris noted that DTE Energy is making steady progress in finalizing contractual agreements to support this data center growth. The utility reported operating earnings of $407 million for Q1 2026, a slight decrease from $436 million in the same period of 2025, attributed to fluctuations in energy trading, tax timing, and elevated interest expenses. However, its electric subsidiary, DTE Electric, experienced an increase in operating earnings to $218 million, up from $147 million year-over-year, driven by favorable weather conditions and successful rate implementations.

The Michigan Public Service Commission granted conditional approval for the Oracle contract in December, paving the way for construction and phased load ramp-up over the next several years. Oracle’s investment includes covering the full cost of its energy and capacity needs, supported by existing capabilities and planned energy storage. In addition, DTE Energy is awaiting approval for a 1-GW Google data center project, which is expected to come online by the end of 2028, bolstered by a mix of renewable generation and energy storage solutions.

Meeting the energy demands of Google could drive an estimated $5 billion in incremental investments in generation and storage through 2032. Harris emphasized that these investments are structured to protect existing customers while providing affordability benefits. The proposed 20-year power supply agreement with Google includes provisions designed to ensure that current customers are not adversely affected by the utility's expansion efforts.

Beyond the Oracle and Google projects, DTE Energy is in advanced discussions concerning additional data center projects that could add 2 GW of incremental load, with even more potential projects identified in the pipeline. As the utility continues to enhance its electric reliability—evidenced by a recent audit and a commitment to modernize its distribution system—DTE Energy remains confident in its ability to meet its long-term operating earnings per share growth target of 6-8% through 2030, supported by ongoing investments and strategic partnerships in the evolving data center landscape.

www.buysellsignals.com

DETROIT, Tuesday, May 05 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $143.50) stock price traded at its 40-day low of $143.0. The last time the price hit that low level was on Thursday, Mar 26, 2026.

DTE Energy Company's stock price fell $3.23 (2.2%) from its previous trading session to close at $143.50.

Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 68.2 points (0.9%) in the day, the relative price change was -3.2%.

There were 3,412,704 shares worth $489.7 million traded today. The fall was exacerbated by strong volume of 2.5 times the average daily volume of 1.3 million shares.

In the past four days trading has been on consistently high volumes; Volume Index (VI) has been 1.2 or higher.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS & PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 394 words

Fri May 1, 2026: DTE Energy Unveils 8.4 GW Data Center Growth Potential

DTE Energy Company is poised to capitalize on significant growth in the data center sector, with plans to support a 1.4-GW Oracle data center currently under construction. On the 1st of May, 2026, company officials revealed that they are also in discussions regarding an additional 7 GW of potential data center projects, indicating a robust pipeline that could reshape the utility's operations in the coming years.

During the company's first quarter earnings call, President and CEO Joi Harris noted that DTE Energy is making steady progress in finalizing contractual agreements to support this data center growth. The utility reported operating earnings of $407 million for Q1 2026, a slight decrease from $436 million in the same period of 2025, attributed to fluctuations in energy trading, tax timing, and elevated interest expenses. However, its electric subsidiary, DTE Electric, experienced an increase in operating earnings to $218 million, up from $147 million year-over-year, driven by favorable weather conditions and successful rate implementations.

The Michigan Public Service Commission granted conditional approval for the Oracle contract in December, paving the way for construction and phased load ramp-up over the next several years. Oracle's investment includes covering the full cost of its energy and capacity needs, supported by existing capabilities and planned energy storage. In addition, DTE Energy is awaiting approval for a 1-GW Google data center project, which is expected to come online by the end of 2028, bolstered by a mix of renewable generation and energy storage solutions.

Meeting the energy demands of Google could drive an estimated $5 billion in incremental investments in generation and storage through 2032. Harris emphasized that these investments are structured to protect existing customers while providing affordability benefits. The proposed 20-year power supply agreement with Google includes provisions designed to ensure that current customers are not adversely affected by the utility's expansion efforts.

Beyond the Oracle and Google projects, DTE Energy is in advanced discussions concerning additional data center projects that could add 2 GW of incremental load, with even more potential projects identified in the pipeline. As the utility continues to enhance its electric reliability—evidenced by a recent audit and a commitment to modernize its distribution system—DTE Energy remains confident in its ability to meet its long-term operating earnings per share growth target of 6-8% through 2030, supported by ongoing investments and strategic partnerships in the evolving data center landscape.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 359 words

Tue Apr 28, 2026: DTE Energy's Rate Freeze Plan Tied to Oracle's Data Centre Development

US utility DTE Energy has announced a plan to pause customer rate increases for two years, contingent upon the successful development of a US$16 billion data centre campus being constructed for tech giant Oracle. This announcement comes as DTE Energy prepares to file for regulatory approval with the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) on 28th April 2026, aiming for a rate freeze that extends through at least 2028.

Joi Harris, president and CEO of DTE Energy, emphasized the importance of the data centre project, stating, 'As long as the first data centre project we're supporting comes online as planned by the end of 2027 and we're able to receive other regulatory approvals, we will refrain from filing another rate request until at least 2028—providing customers two years without an increase in rates after the current request is complete.'

The two data centre contracts associated with the Oracle project are projected to contribute approximately US$9 billion to enhancements in DTE's electric system by 2045, thereby alleviating cost pressures on other customers. The utility's upcoming request for US$474.3 million will support significant investments in the electric grid and power generation, including the conversion of the Belle River Power Plant from coal to natural gas and the establishment of the 220MW/880MWh Trenton Channel Energy Center, which is expected to become the largest stand-alone battery energy storage facility in the Great Lakes region upon completion.

DTE Energy's construction of the battery energy storage system (BESS) commenced in 2024, following MPSC approval for the Trenton project. The utility had originally partnered with Powin for the BESS technology but shifted to LG Energy Solution after experiencing delays and a contract termination with Powin in June 2025. This strategic pivot is aimed at achieving a cost reduction of over US$30 million for the project.

The financing for Related Digital's data centre, which will utilize DTE Energy's power, involves equity from Related Digital and funds associated with Blackstone, alongside long-term fixed-rate debt anchored by Pacific Investment Management Company (PIMCO). The project is anticipated to generate significant economic benefits, including job creation and tax revenue for local communities, while also reinforcing DTE Energy's commitment to renewable energy solutions.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 298 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: City Opera House Partners with DTE Energy for Sustainable Initiatives

TRAVERSE CITY — The City Opera House has named DTE Energy as its first green sponsor to support environmentally conscious operations. As of 24th April 2026, this partnership marks a significant step towards implementing sustainability initiatives within the organization.

The collaboration aims to enhance eco-friendly practices, including the transition to rechargeable systems for microphone packs, which will eliminate the need for disposable batteries. Additionally, the City Opera House is adopting recycled copy paper throughout its operations, encompassing all printed program inserts. Future plans involve installing a bike rack to promote alternative transportation options for both patrons and staff.

Chad Lindsey, the executive director of the City Opera House, emphasized the importance of this partnership, stating, “DTE Energy's partnership helps us make thoughtful, earth-conscious decisions that align with our values as a historic community anchor. Together, we're ensuring that our operations reflect not only artistic excellence but environmental responsibility.” This sentiment underscores the venue's commitment to integrating sustainability into its core mission.

Henry Decker, vice president of gas sales and supply at DTE Energy, reiterated the company's dedication to sustainability, remarking, “We look forward to supporting the City Opera House as they continue to make a meaningful difference for the environment and for the people they serve.” DTE Energy is known for its initiatives that help customers reduce their carbon footprints, such as the CleanVision Natural Gas Balance program, which balances carbon emissions from natural gas use by supporting Michigan forest management and the development of renewable natural gas.

The City Opera House serves as a cultural hub for NorthernMichigan and remains committed to reducing its environmental footprint. The collaboration with DTE Energy is a promising step towards fostering a more sustainable future, reflecting a growing trend among cultural institutions to prioritize environmental responsibility alongside their artistic missions.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 72 words

Operational Veteran Joi Harris Elected CEO to Navigate Decarbonization and Grid Challenges

Joi Harris, formerly President and COO, has been appointed CEO and President of DTE Energy, succeeding Jerry Norcia. Her extensive operational experience is expected to guide the company through its decarbonization strategy and grid modernization challenges. While her appointment is viewed as a move for continuity, she faces significant regulatory scrutiny over rates and service quality, and her performance will be measured by her ability to meet growth targets amidst these pressures.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 70 words

DTE Energy Outperforms Q3 Expectations Despite Revenue Headwinds, Reaffirms Guidance

DTE Energy reported Q3 2025 operating earnings of $2.25 per share, surpassing estimates despite a nearly $1 billion revenue shortfall caused by lower fuel cost pass-throughs. The earnings beat was driven by cost management and tax credit timing in the DTE Electric segment. Management reaffirmed its full-year guidance, signaling financial stability, but also noted that future growth will depend more on industrial and data center demand rather than residential load.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

MPSC Rejection of Saline Data Center Contract Signals Regulatory Headwinds for Tech Load Growth

DTE Energy's plan to build a major data center in Saline Township was blocked after the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) failed to approve a special contract before a key deadline. The project was central to DTE's strategy of offsetting stagnant residential demand. The rejection, based on concerns about cost-shifting to residential customers and grid stability, sets a challenging precedent for future large-scale industrial projects and introduces significant execution risk to DTE's growth thesis.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 63 words

Regulator Authorizes $217 Million Rate Increase, Capping Revenue Below Utility Request

The MPSC approved a $217.4 million rate increase for DTE Electric, which was only 48% of the company's original request. While the revenue supports grid modernization and near-term financials, the significant reduction signals a tightening regulatory environment. This outcome forces DTE to file for rate increases more frequently to recover its infrastructure investments, a strategy that risks creating friction with regulators and consumers.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 67 words

DTE Files $574 Million Rate Case Amidst Heightened Political Scrutiny

Shortly after its last rate adjustment, DTE Energy filed for another increase, requesting $574.1 million to fund a $4.4 billion investment in grid hardening and cleaner energy. The request has drawn immediate political opposition, notably from the Michigan Attorney General. The outcome is critical for DTE's ability to earn its authorized return and execute its reliability improvement strategy, but a significant disallowance could threaten its growth targets.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 64 words

Investment Plan Expanded by $6.5 Billion to Support Grid Modernization and Load Growth

DTE Energy has increased its five-year capital investment plan by $6.5 billion to accelerate grid modernization and expand renewable generation. The expansion is intended to support a 6-8% annual EPS growth rate, driven by projected load increases from electrification and data centers. However, this large-scale plan faces significant execution risk, including the need for regulatory approval for cost recovery and potential supply chain constraints.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 58 words

Commissioning of Polaris Solar Park Advances Voluntary Green Power Strategy

DTE Energy has brought the 100-megawatt Polaris Solar Park into commercial operation. As a key part of its MIGreenPower program, the facility allows corporate customers to meet their ESG goals. The project's success demonstrates DTE's ability to execute utility-scale solar developments, although the scalability of this model faces future challenges related to land availability and local community opposition.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 63 words

Construction Begins on Dedicated Solar Array for Ford Motor Company

DTE has started construction on a utility-scale solar park dedicated to supplying power to Ford Motor Company's EV manufacturing operations. This project secures long-term revenue from a major industrial partner and mitigates the risk of load defection. While it reinforces DTE's "energy-as-a-service" model, it also ties a portion of the utility's performance to the cyclical automotive industry and Ford's specific EV strategy success.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 65 words

Conversion of Belle River Plant Commences, Pivoting Coal Assets to Peaker Role

DTE has begun converting a coal-fired unit at its Belle River Power Plant to a natural gas peaker plant. The $154 million project is a strategic step to ensure grid reliability as the company retires coal plants and integrates more renewables. This conversion repurposes an existing asset to maintain resource adequacy cost-effectively, though the long-term viability of gas assets remains subject to future environmental policy.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 68 words

Advanced Negotiations for 3 GW Data Center Load Define Long-Term Growth Thesis

DTE Energy is in advanced negotiations to supply approximately 3 GW of power to prospective data center clients, a volume that could transform its commercial load profile. This potential demand is central to the company's long-term growth strategy. However, realizing this pipeline is contingent on overcoming significant regulatory hurdles related to cost allocation and ensuring sufficient generation capacity to serve such a large load without compromising grid reliability.

SECTION 4 RECENT NEWS

The utility reported operating earnings of $407 million for Q1 2026, a slight decrease from $436 million in the same period of 2025, attributed to fluctuations in energy trading, tax timing, and elevated interest expenses. However, its electric subsidiary, DTE Electric, experienced an increase in operating earnings to $218 million, up from $147 million year-over-year, driven by favorable weather conditions and successful rate implementations.

The Michigan Public Service Commission granted conditional approval for the Oracle contract in December, paving the way for construction and phased load ramp-up over the next several years. Oracle’s investment includes covering the full cost of its energy and capacity needs, supported by existing capabilities and planned energy storage. In addition, DTE Energy is awaiting approval for a 1-GW Google data center project, which is expected to come online by the end of 2028, bolstered by a mix of renewable generation and energy storage solutions.

Meeting the energy demands of Google could drive an estimated $5 billion in incremental investments in generation and storage through 2032. Harris emphasized that these investments are structured to protect existing customers while providing affordability benefits. The proposed 20-year power supply agreement with Google includes provisions designed to ensure that current customers are not adversely affected by the utility's expansion efforts.

Beyond the Oracle and Google projects, DTE Energy is in advanced discussions concerning additional data center projects that could add 2 GW of incremental load, with even more potential projects identified in the pipeline. As the utility continues to enhance its electric reliability—evidenced by a recent audit and a commitment to modernize its distribution system—DTE Energy remains confident in its ability to meet its long-term operating earnings per share growth target of 6-8% through 2030, supported by ongoing investments and strategic partnerships in the evolving data center landscape.

urn:contentItem:6JGY-P943-RVSH-W0TX-00000-00DTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Alliant Energy Corporation *Updated*2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisDTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Alliant Energy Corporation *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy's trailing twelve months dividend is $4.51. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.97%. Its current price is at a 1.1% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 21.1% premium to its 52 week low. If DTE Energy was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.6%.

In comparison, Alliant Energy Corporation's trailing twelve months dividend is $2.08. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.84%. Its current price is at a 0.4% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 4.8% premium to its 52 week low. If Alliant Energy Corporation was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 2.97%.

$1000 invested into DTE Energy stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,679 today. This total gain of $679 is generated by a capital gain of $456 and dividends of $223.

$1000 invested into Alliant Energy Corporation stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,768 today. This total gain of $768 is generated by a capital gain of $525 and dividends of $244.

DTE Energy's dividend has grown 7.1% over the last 4 years.

Alliant Energy Corporation's dividend has grown 5.8% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

DTE Energy, headquartered in Detroit, operates as a diversified energy company with its primary focus on regulated electric and natural gas utilities. The company serves 2.3 million electric customers in Southeast Michigan and 1.4 million gas customers statewide, making these utilities its largest revenue sources. Its portfolio also includes non-utility businesses specializing in custom energy solutions, renewable energy generation, and energy marketing and trading. The fastest-growing segments include clean energy development, such as solar parks under the MIGreenPower program, and hyperscale data center contracts like the 1.4 GW Oracle agreement. These initiatives drive economic progress, affordability benefits, and advancements toward net-zero goals.

Company Profile

Alliant Energy Corporation is a regulated utility holding company. It provides electric and natural gas services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in the Midwest. The company operates through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its primary revenue stream is electric utility operations, which constituted about 93% of third-quarter 2025 revenues. This segment is also its fastest-growing, with revenues up 13% year-over-year, driven by rate increases, higher sales volumes, and expanding data center demand. Gas utility revenues represent approximately 4% of the total, with other utility and non-utility segments comprising the rest. The gas segment also showed growth, increasing 4% amid seasonal fluctuations.

urn:contentItem:6JGH-RVD3-RS9K-G47H-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: DTE Energy sequential quarterly profits hit by 64% fall Corporate Wire Date: 02 May 2026 04:34 EDT Release Date: April 30, 20262026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: DTE Energy sequential quarterly profits hit by 64% fall Corporate Wire Date: 02 May 2026 04:34 EDT Release Date: April 30, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE), announced net profit of $247m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 64% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 44% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Total revenue was up 15.8% from $4.4b to $5.1b

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 44.5% from $445.0m to $247.0m

- EPS slumped 44.4% from $2.14 to $1.19

- EBITDA to Revenues was down from 19.8% to 13.1%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 10% to 4.8%

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 9.6% to 3.7%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 9.6% to 3.7%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was down from 10.2% to 9.4%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

ACTIVITIES:

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market.

MARKET POSITION:

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, climbed $17.35 (or 13.2%) in the trailing 12 months to May 01, 2026 to close at $148.79. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 29.8% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -16.6%. Average daily volume of 1.3 million shares in the trailing year was 1.1 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JGP-DBK3-SFJ4-5463-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: DTE Energy reports 31% sequential fall in Quarterly Revenue2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: DTE Energy reports 31% sequential fall in Quarterly Revenue

AMERICAN RESULTS

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE), announced total revenue of $5.1b for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 31% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 16% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Total revenue was up 15.8% from $4.4b to $5.1b

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 44.5% from $445.0m to $247.0m

- EPS slumped 44.4% from $2.14 to $1.19

- EBITDA to Revenues was down from 19.8% to 13.1%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 10% to 4.8%

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 9.6% to 3.7%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 9.6% to 3.7%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was down from 10.2% to 9.4%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 44.4%, Revenue was up 15.8% and Net Profit was down 44.5%.

ACTIVITIES:

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market.

MARKET POSITION:

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, climbed $17.35 (or 13.2%) in the trailing 12 months to May 01, 2026 to close at $148.79. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 29.8% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -16.6%. Average daily volume of 1.3 million shares in the trailing year was 1.1 times average daily volume in the previous year.

value.Title: Contrasting CMS Energy NYSE: CMS Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale OTCMKTS:TEZNY

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-BJ13-SFCK-N1WD-00000-00Contrasting CMS Energy NYSE:CMS Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale OTCMKTS:TEZNY2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisContrasting CMS Energy NYSE:CMS Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale OTCMKTS:TEZNY

CMS Energy NYSE:CMS - Get Free Report and Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale OTCMKTS:TEZNY - Get Free Report are both large-cap utilities companies, but which is the superior stock? We will contrast the two businesses based on the strength of their institutional ownership, risk, valuation, dividends, analyst recommendations, profitability and earnings.

Institutional and Insider Ownership

93.6% of CMS Energy shares are held by institutional investors. 0.5% of CMS Energy shares are held by company insiders. Strong institutional ownership is an indication that large money managers, endowments and hedge funds believe a stock is poised for long-term growth.

Dividends

CMS Energy pays an annual dividend of $2.28 per share and has a dividend yield of 3.0%. Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale pays an annual dividend of $0.47 per share and has a dividend yield of 1.3%. CMS Energy pays out 63.0% of its earnings in the form of a dividend. CMS Energy has increased its dividend for 3 consecutive years. CMS Energy is clearly the better dividend stock, given its higher yield and longer track record of dividend growth.

Earnings Valuation

This table compares CMS Energy and Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale"s revenue, earnings per share and valuation. Gross RevenuePrice/Sales RatioNet IncomeEarnings Per SharePrice/Earnings RatioCMS Energy$8.82 billion2.66$1.07 billion$3.6221.01Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale$3.98 billion6.07$1.15 billionN/AN/A

Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale has lower revenue, but higher earnings than CMS Energy.

Risk and Volatility

CMS Energy has a beta of 0.37, suggesting that its share price is 63% less volatile than the S 500. Comparatively, Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale has a beta of 0.68, suggesting that its share price is 32% less volatile than the S 500.

Analyst Ratings

This is a summary of recent ratings and price targets for CMS Energy and Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale, as provided by MarketBeat. Sell RatingsHold RatingsBuy RatingsStrong Buy RatingsRating ScoreCMS Energy05712.69Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale11001.50

CMS Energy presently has a consensus target price of $81.50, suggesting a potential upside of 7.16%. Given CMS Energy's stronger consensus rating and higher possible upside, research analysts plainly believe CMS Energy is more favorable than Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale.

Profitability

This table compares CMS Energy and Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale's net margins, return on equity and return on assets. Net MarginsReturn on EquityReturn on AssetsCMS Energy12.55%12.17%2.91%Terna Rete Elettrica NazionaleN/AN/AN/A

Summary

CMS Energy beats Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale on 12 of the 16 factors compared between the two stocks.

About CMS Energy Get Free Report

CMS Energy Corporation operates as an energy company primarily in Michigan. The company operates through three segments: Electric Utility; Gas Utility; and Enterprises. The Electric Utility segment is involved in the generation, purchase, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity. This segment generates electricity through coal, wind, gas, renewable energy, oil, and nuclear sources. Its distribution system comprises 208 miles of high-voltage distribution overhead lines; 4 miles of high-voltage distribution underground lines; 4,428 miles of high-voltage distribution overhead lines; 19 miles of high-voltage distribution underground lines; 82,474 miles of electric distribution overhead lines; 9,395 miles of underground distribution lines; 1,093 substations; and 3 battery facilities. The Gas Utility segment engages in the purchase, transmission, storage, distribution, and sale of natural gas, which includes 2,392 miles of transmission lines; 15 gas storage fields; 28,065 miles of distribution mains; and 8 compressor stations. The Enterprises segment is involved in the independent power production and marketing, including the development and operation of renewable generation. It serves 1.9 million electric and 1.8 million gas customers, including residential, commercial, and diversified industrial customers. The company was incorporated in 1987 and is headquartered in Jackson, Michigan.

About Terna Rete Elettrica Nazionale Get Free Report

Terna S.p.A., together with its subsidiaries, provides electricity transmission and dispatching services in Italy, other Euro-area countries, and internationally. It operates through Regulated, Non-Regulated, and International segments. The company is involved in the design, construction, management, development, operation, and maintenance of national transmission grid; dispatching and metering; and construction of storage systems. It also supplies transformers and cables; provides energy and connectivity services; and installs and operates interconnecting lines. In addition, the company designs, produces, commercializes, and repairs power transformers for electricity transmission and distribution grids; industrial transformers for the steel and metals industries; special transformers for convertors used in electrochemical production; produces and sells marine and terrestrial cables; develops renewable energy projects; and undertakes private interconnector projects. Terna S.p.A. was founded in 1962 and is based in Rome, Italy.
