value.Title: IKEA GROUP - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGW-JGM3-S63C-23M3-00000-00IKEA GROUP - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ STYLISH AND SMART: IKEA DEBUTS STARKVIND AS AN AIR PURIFIER AND END TABLE

IKEA has introduced the Starkvind, a dual purpose device that acts as a smart air purifier and a side table, intended to elevate indoor air quality without sacrificing aesthetics. The Starkvind is part of the Home Smart range and is due to launch on 13th October 2021.Designed to fit contemporary living spaces, the unit can be controlled through the Tradfri Gateway and IKEA app, combining convenience with a clean look.It features a three stage filtration system and five fan speeds, including an auto mode that responds to real time air quality readings. A built in sensor provides continuous updates via the companion app, helping users monitor air health.A standalone purifier option is also available for customers who prefer a more traditional setup, ensuring choices for different interiors.

+ KUWAIT IKEA NAMED EIGHT-TIME SERVICE HERO FOR CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE EXCELLENCE

IKEA Kuwait has earned the Best Customer Service in the Furniture category from the Service Hero Customer Satisfaction Index (SHCSI), marking its eighth win. The announcement came on the 21st of April, 2026, reflecting IKEA's sustained focus on customer experience in Kuwait.SHCSI relies entirely on independent feedback from customers across more than 900 private sector companies. This year’s Kuwait index stood at 84.0 points, up from 78.9 a year earlier, highlighting strengths in reliability, convenience, staff performance, digital experience, and overall value for money."At IKEA, we are deeply committed to creating inspiring and seamless experiences in our stores, while offering an equally simple and engaging journey online and through the IKEA app," said Marino Maganto, CEO for Kuwait, Jordan and Morocco. The company continues to support customers with online planning and financing options, plus fast delivery and assembly. An in-store ceremony honored co-workers, with Maganto adding that "together we can make a positive impact on people’s lives" and that happy co-workers translate to better service.

+ IKEA EXPANDS DETROIT FOOTPRINT WITH COMPACT, COMMUNITY-ORIENTED STORE

IKEA Group is set to open its second Michigan location this summer in the Detroit area, marking a move toward a more accessible urban footprint. The store will be smaller than typical IKEA sites and will feature a curated assortment tailored to local preferences. The announcement, made on the 21st of April 2026, underscores the company’s focus on urban demand.The compact format is designed to simplify shopping while preserving IKEA's affordability and Scandinavian design ethos.The expansion aligns with broader shifts toward localized options and urban revitalization, with IKEA aiming to boost accessibility and engage community shoppers as the project moves forward.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) USES GEMINI AI TO ENHANCE INSTORE AND PHONE SUPPORT

As of 28th April 2026, Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is deploying AI-powered phone agents built on Google Cloud's Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience to enable customers to state their needs in their own words when calling a storeThe AI system is designed to rapidly understand inquiries yet preserve the option for direct human interaction. The company expects the technology to reduce call queue pressure and allow associates to devote more attention to customers in the storeGemini was selected for its advanced conversational understanding and intent detection. The AI models integrate with Home Depot’s existing systems to deliver a seamless customer-facing experience, with understanding in ten seconds and often resolution in a single interaction. Early pilot feedback points to improved associate satisfaction as frontline staff spend more time assisting shoppersHome Depot intends to roll out the technology to all U.S. stores by the end of 2026, continuing to refine the system based on customer feedback and user experience data

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) REPORTS OPENING OF SAN MARCOS STORE BACKED BY $13 MILLION INVESTMENT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) is expanding into San Marcos with a $13 million store project confirmed on 28th April 2026. This will be the retailer’s first store in the college town, which has seen rapid growth in housing and retail. Plans filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) indicate a 135,918-square-foot footprint, including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction scheduled to start in September 2026 and to finish by August 2027. The new location sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and slated to open in October 2026. San Marcos, on the I-35 corridor and home to Texas State University, continues to attract development, aligning with Home Depot to broaden its presence. The company noted a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations nationwide

+ MACY'S, INC. (NYSE:M) DEPLOYS GOOGLE GEMINI BASED SHOPPING ASSISTANT AS PART OF AI INITIATIVE

During the Google Cloud Next 2026 conference in Las Vegas on 23rd April 2026, AutoZone, The Home Depot, Macy's and Ulta Beauty announced partnerships with Google to introduce agentic AI capabilities across their platforms. The move seeks to extend Google AI across both online and in-store experiences.AutoZone, Inc. (NYSE:AZO) completed a full-stack migration to Google Cloud, planning to retire legacy data centers within three years. The migration is designed to boost performance and flexibility, with Google AI Cloud supporting system monitoring and automated high-volume workflows to lift productivity.The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) will roll out AI-powered phone agents based on Google Cloud Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience, enabling natural language conversations and reducing navigational friction for customers. Early pilots show inquiries resolved in under ten seconds, with a U.S. store rollout in the coming year. Ulta Beauty, Inc. (NASDAQ:ULTA) is launching Ulta AI and pursuing the Universal Commerce Protocol for agentic AI-based commerce to expand seamless beauty discovery across digital channels, per Lauren Brindley.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) NAMES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO HEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined in 2003 as a Pottery Barn district manager and rose through the Rejuvenation banner to become senior vice president in 2020.In her new role, she will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives to drive Rejuvenation within the overall brand portfolio.CEO Laura Alber noted that Taylor's leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to reach billion-dollar potential, reflecting her track record and design-oriented approach.Rejuvenation has expanded with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has partnered on a Roll & Hill lighting collection. Williams-Sonoma has named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The firm projects 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) EVERCORE SETS $200 TARGET AND KEEPS HOLD RATING

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) maintains a Hold rating as Evercore raises its price target to $200 from $195 in a 14th April 2026 report. Analyst Oliver Wintermantel, who leads Williams-Sonoma coverage at Evercore, has a 66.7% success rate and a 23.9% average return over the past year, according to TipRanks. The target adjustment reflects Evercore’s view of market dynamics and Williams-Sonoma’s operational strategies within a competitive home-goods landscape. While the rating remains Hold, the new target suggests investors should monitor seasonal trends, inventory management and supply-chain efficiency as Williams-Sonoma pursues its projected performance.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) GOLDMAN SACHS BOOSTS RATING TO BUY WITH HIGHER PRICE TARGET

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a constructive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.The investment bank projects Williams-Sonoma to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates, while non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast at $13.00, down 33.82%. The update aligns with a broader trend of reassessing the home goods sector as consumer spending evolves.Investors will monitor how Williams-Sonoma translates this outlook into stronger revenue growth and margin discipline in the coming year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ SPANAWAY HUB PURCHASE SIGNALS AMAZON'S GROWING E-COMMERCE LOGISTICS PUSH

Amazon has bought Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—about 1.1 million square feet along with nearby parcels—for more than $200 million, as reported on the 27th of April 2026.The move strengthens Amazon's logistics network in the Pacific Northwest, aiding faster deliveries as e-commerce grows.Ashley Furniture is divesting the property as part of a strategic realignment, leaving questions about its future distribution plans.The sale spotlights the brisk commercial real estate activity in the Pacific Northwest and Amazon's continued push into large-scale warehousing.

+ FRONTLINEIQ DEBUTS INDUSTRY-LEADING AI COACHING FOR IN-PERSON SALES AT HIGH POINT

HIGH POINT—FrontlineIQ(TradeMark), the AI sales coaching platform for in-person teams, announced the Ashley AI Advantage, developed with Ashley Furniture Industries, at the High Point Spring Market on the 27th of April, 2026.The program introduces interactive AI role-play and real-time conversation coaching for frontline associates, enabling practice with real customer interactions and instant feedback based on proven selling methods.The Ashley AI Advantage is free for Ashley Wholesale customers and licensees, with scalable options. Ben Rodier, CEO of FrontlineIQ, called it transformative, while Ashley's John Mask said it offers a new level of showroom-floor confidence and consistency.As a turnkey entry point pre-configured for home furnishings, the platform can be customized for retailers' training processes. More information is available at www.frontlineiq.ai/ashley.

+ ORNATE HOME GAINS OPERATIONAL INSIGHTS FROM ASHLEY’S ADVANCE HUB IN NORTH CAROLINA

SANTA ANA, CA – Ornate Home's founder Mehmet Uncuoglu completed a tour of Ashley Furniture Industries’ Advance, North Carolina facility on the 21st of April, 2026, as part of ongoing efforts to strengthen ties with the world’s largest furniture maker. The visit offered firsthand insights into large-scale production and distribution that will inform service at Ornate Home's Santa Ana and Charlotte showrooms. The tour covered production floors, logistics, quality controls, and supply chain coordination at Ashley’s Advance hub, a major U.S. manufacturing and distribution center, with Ashley employing more than 35,000 people and operating over 30 million square feet of space globally. 'The Ashley team’s hospitality and transparency during our visit was remarkable,' Uncuoglu said. 'Seeing their commitment to quality control and efficiency reinforces why Ashley furniture and mattress products remain cornerstone offerings in our showrooms.' The visit aligns with Ornate Home's expansion plans; the Charlotte location benefits from a regional link to Ashley's North Carolina operations, enabling faster delivery times. Ornate Home operates as an Ashley furniture store serving Santa Ana and Charlotte; Founded in 2021, it offers same-day white-glove delivery in Orange County and ships nationwide from company-owned warehouses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORES ACROSS GROWTH MARKETS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Expands Footprint With New Stores Across Growth MarketsLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space and a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, with more than 100 associates on staff. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will showcase redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms that blend style, affordability and innovation. New showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Other openings are planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. The expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet growing demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. District Manager Christian Redman highlighted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is positioned to deliver value, convenience and service to the community. Investors will assess how these locations contribute to sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) OPENS 94,000-SQ.-FT PORT ST. LUCIE STORE AS PART OF GROWTH PLAN

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) unveils expansion plans including a Port St. Lucie location in Florida sized at about 94,000 square feet with a 30,000-square-foot garden center and more than 100 staff, slated to open in early June 2026. The company also plans four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky to begin operations later this year.The new stores will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for quick paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup to enhance convenience for customers.Lowe's operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, and handles roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales exceeded $83 billion. The MyLowe's Rewards program provides points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, reinforcing customer loyalty

+ LOWE’S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) ADDS FOUR STORES ACROSS FLORIDA, TEXAS AND KENTUCKY AS PART OF EXPANSION

Apr. 21, 2026 Lowe’s Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) plans a Florida expansion with the Port St. Lucie store, which will be Lowe’s 133rd location in the state. The store encompasses about 94,000 square feet, including a 30,000-square-foot garden center, and is slated to open in early June.The Port St. Lucie outlet is expected to create over 100 jobs, reinforcing Lowe’s commitment to communities in growing areas. In addition to Port St. Lucie, Lowe’s will launch openings in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky, broadening its national footprint to more than 1,700 stores across North America. The retailer will also modernize kitchen and appliance showrooms to enhance the shopping experience across new and select existing locations in 2026, as part of a broader modernization initiative. Christian Redman, Lowe’s district manager, said, "Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly, and we’re excited to support our neighbors and local businesses with everything they need for projects big and small."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) BETS ON SPEED WITH NATIONWIDE NEXT-DAY DELIVERY EXPANSION AFTER 2025 SALES DECLINE

As Of The 28th Of April 2026, Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Expands Next-Day Delivery To 60% Of The U.S. Population, Reaching Over 50 Metro Areas, With Many Deliveries FreeThe Move Emphasizes Speed In Online Shopping And Supports Target's Efforts To Rebuild Shopper TrustEligible Items Rose To 85% Of All In-Store Selections, With Orders Placed By 6 P.M. Local Time Arriving The Next Day; Standard Delivery Is $5.99, And Orders Over $35 Qualify For Free DeliveryThe Initiative Is Part Of The New Chapter $5 Billion Strategy Led By Chief Executive Officer Michael Fiddelke, Following A 1.7% Net Sales Decline In 2025, And Includes Store Refreshes And New Collaborations

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) NON-MERCHANDISE REVENUE SURGES 25% IN QUARTER

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) posts fiscal 2025 results for the period ending 31st January 2026, with stock up 29% since the start of 2026.\n\nE-commerce sales reach 20.6% of total merchandise sales, and the fourth quarter e-commerce share rose to 23.7%.\n\nNon-merchandise sales rose 25% in the quarter, with membership revenue more than doubling year over year. Target operates as a general merchandise retailer with stores and digital platforms, delivering a mix of everyday essentials and differentiated merchandise to customers. Analysts note that opportunities in AI and other sectors may offer upside as market dynamics evolve

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) PARTNERS WITH LOCAL NONPROFITS TO REVITALIZE COMMUNITY SPACES ACROSS 13 CITIES

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) expands its Bullseye Builds with Community Program, a design-driven effort to revitalize community spaces in 13 U.S. cities with a $1 million investment. The initiative will begin on 21st May 2026 in Denver and Las VegasLaunched in 2024, the program has completed 25 projects to date and supported more than 250,000 community members with help from Target volunteers. In Denver, Trevista at Horace Mann Elementary School will upgrade the student wellness room and create outdoor learning spaces; In Las Vegas, The Just One Project will address food insecurity and improve shopping areas and pantry facilities. Each project will involve more than 100 volunteers from TargetOther cities include Austin, Birmingham, Columbus, Jersey City, Logan Township, Orlando, Washington, D.C., Minneapolis-St. Paul, Phoenix, St. Louis, and Seattle. "At Target, we believe investing in the places we call home builds strong communities and strong partnerships," said Kiera Fernandez, Executive Vice President and Chief Community and Stakeholder Engagement Officer. The company also notes that team members contributed 1 million volunteer hours in the U.S. in 2025

urn:contentItem:6JK1-XSF3-SHSN-V483-00000-00Past Week: DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) down 3.4% in 2 weeks; -$5.03 [-3.4%]2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisPast Week: DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) down 3.4% in 2 weeks; -$5.03 [-3.4%]

NYSE END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE $142.0), the NYSE's 7th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, extended its slide in the past week ended Wednesday, shedding an additional 44.0c (or 0.3%) to close at $142.0. The shares have dropped $5.03 (or 3.4%) over the past two weeks. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 308.3 points (or 4.3%) in the two weeks, this represented a relative price change of -7.7%.

The price ranged between a low of $142.0 on Wednesday May 13 and a high of $142.58 on Tuesday May 12.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

Quarterly Report (Q1/2026):

Release Date: April 30, 2026

Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: DTE Energy sequential quarterly profits hit by 64% fall

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE), announced net profit of $247m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 64% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 44% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 44.4%, Revenue was up 15.8% and Net Profit was down 44.5%.

Wednesday's price drop

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) in negative territory today, declining 0.4%

DETROIT, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $141.99) stock price declined 59.50c (0.4%) from Tuesday's close of $141.99. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 55.0 points (0.7%) in the day, the relative price change was -1.2%. There were 1,124,177 shares worth $159.6 million traded today; the volume was 0.8 times the average daily trading volume of 1.4 million shares.

The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $149.51 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $136.69, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $136.69, a third bullish indicator.

SECTION 7 CORPORATE PROFILE

7.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

Wed May 13, 2026: DTE Energy Company Secures Board Re-election Amid Shareholder Meeting

DTE Energy Company has successfully concluded its Annual Meeting, where significant decisions were made regarding its governance structure. On the 7th May 2026, shareholders elected all director nominees as outlined in the Proxy, marking a pivotal moment for the company's leadership. The newly elected board members, who will serve a one-year term expiring in 2027, include notable figures such as Nicholas K. Akins, David A. Brandon, and Gail J. McGovern, among others.

In addition to the board elections, the shareholders ratified the appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for the year 2026. This decision underscores DTE Energy Company's commitment to transparency and accuracy in financial reporting, crucial factors in maintaining investor trust and confidence in the company's operations.

Furthermore, shareholders approved the overall executive compensation for the company's named executive officers on an advisory basis. This endorsement reflects a strong level of confidence in DTE Energy Company's leadership and the compensation strategies as detailed in the Proxy. The approval indicates that shareholders are satisfied with how the company is navigating the evolving energy landscape.

The outcomes of the Annual Meeting highlight DTE Energy Company's ongoing efforts to align its governance and executive compensation with shareholder interests. By ensuring that its leadership is elected in accordance with shareholder preferences, DTE Energy aims to foster continued growth and stability in the energy sector, which remains a vital component of the economy.

For investors considering DTE Energy Company, understanding the business's profitability and stock valuations is essential. Prospective investors should evaluate the company's performance while staying alert to any potential warning signs. In-depth research on DTE Energy Co (DTE) may provide valuable insights into the investment's viability.

Recent News 2 of 5, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 335 words

Tue May 12, 2026: DTE Energy Secures Board Approval Amidst Growing Investor Confidence

DTE Energy Co. (NYSE:DTE) reported that shareholders elected all 13 nominated directors to its board and approved other proposals at the company's annual meeting held on 13th May 2026. This decisive support reflects the confidence investors have in DTE Energy's governance and strategic direction.

Among those elected to serve one-year terms expiring in 2027 are Nicholas K. Akins, David A. Brandon, and Deborah L. Byers, among others. Each nominee received significant backing, with at least 149 million votes in favor, while the number of votes withheld ranged from approximately 482,000 to over 6.4 million. The strong shareholder support comes as DTE Energy maintains its reputation as a reliable dividend payer, having sustained dividend payments for 56 consecutive years, with a current yield of 3.27%.

Shareholders also ratified the appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as DTE Energy's independent registered public accounting firm for 2026. This proposal garnered 174,185,300 votes in favor and reflects the trust in the company's financial oversight. Additionally, shareholders approved, on an advisory basis, the compensation of the company's named executive officers, with 149,823,374 votes in favor, indicating a favorable view of executive performance.

In recent financial updates, DTE Energy reported first-quarter adjusted earnings per share of $1.95, which fell short of the consensus estimate of $2.03, primarily due to timing issues in its Energy Trading segment. Despite this setback, management reaffirmed its guidance and expressed confidence in achieving an 8% or higher earnings per share growth from 2027 through 2030, bolstered by firming hyperscaler contracts. The company has also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.165 per share, payable on 15th July 2026.

Analysts have adjusted their price targets for DTE Energy in light of these developments. Mizuho raised its target to $165 from $155 while maintaining an Outperform rating. BofA Securities also increased its target to $162 from $155, reflecting a Buy rating. Conversely, Jefferies lowered its target to $168 from $170 but retained a Buy rating, citing various factors that reduce risk. These adjustments highlight ongoing confidence in DTE Energy's growth trajectory.

Recent News 3 of 5, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 394 words

Fri May 1, 2026: DTE Energy Unveils 8.4 GW Data Center Growth Potential

DTE Energy Company is poised to capitalize on significant growth in the data center sector, with plans to support a 1.4-GW Oracle data center currently under construction. On the 1st of May, 2026, company officials revealed that they are also in discussions regarding an additional 7 GW of potential data center projects, indicating a robust pipeline that could reshape the utility's operations in the coming years.

During the company's first quarter earnings call, President and CEO Joi Harris noted that DTE Energy is making steady progress in finalizing contractual agreements to support this data center growth. The utility reported operating earnings of $407 million for Q1 2026, a slight decrease from $436 million in the same period of 2025, attributed to fluctuations in energy trading, tax timing, and elevated interest expenses. However, its electric subsidiary, DTE Electric, experienced an increase in operating earnings to $218 million, up from $147 million year-over-year, driven by favorable weather conditions and successful rate implementations.

The Michigan Public Service Commission granted conditional approval for the Oracle contract in December, paving the way for construction and phased load ramp-up over the next several years. Oracle's investment includes covering the full cost of its energy and capacity needs, supported by existing capabilities and planned energy storage. In addition, DTE Energy is awaiting approval for a 1-GW Google data center project, which is expected to come online by the end of 2028, bolstered by a mix of renewable generation and energy storage solutions.

Meeting the energy demands of Google could drive an estimated $5 billion in incremental investments in generation and storage through 2032. Harris emphasized that these investments are structured to protect existing customers while providing affordability benefits. The proposed 20-year power supply agreement with Google includes provisions designed to ensure that current customers are not adversely affected by the utility's expansion efforts.

Beyond the Oracle and Google projects, DTE Energy is in advanced discussions concerning additional data center projects that could add 2 GW of incremental load, with even more potential projects identified in the pipeline. As the utility continues to enhance its electric reliability—evidenced by a recent audit and a commitment to modernize its distribution system—DTE Energy remains confident in its ability to meet its long-term operating earnings per share growth target of 6-8% through 2030, supported by ongoing investments and strategic partnerships in the evolving data center landscape.

Recent News 4 of 5, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 359 words

Tue Apr 28, 2026: DTE Energy's Rate Freeze Plan Tied to Oracle's Data Centre Development

US utility DTE Energy has announced a plan to pause customer rate increases for two years, contingent upon the successful development of a US$16 billion data centre campus being constructed for tech giant Oracle. This announcement comes as DTE Energy prepares to file for regulatory approval with the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) on 28th April 2026, aiming for a rate freeze that extends through at least 2028.

Joi Harris, president and CEO of DTE Energy, emphasized the importance of the data centre project, stating, 'As long as the first data centre project we're supporting comes online as planned by the end of 2027 and we're able to receive other regulatory approvals, we will refrain from filing another rate request until at least 2028—providing customers two years without an increase in rates after the current request is complete.'

The two data centre contracts associated with the Oracle project are projected to contribute approximately US$9 billion to enhancements in DTE's electric system by 2045, thereby alleviating cost pressures on other customers. The utility's upcoming request for US$474.3 million will support significant investments in the electric grid and power generation, including the conversion of the Belle River Power Plant from coal to natural gas and the establishment of the 220MW/880MWh Trenton Channel Energy Center, which is expected to become the largest stand-alone battery energy storage facility in the Great Lakes region upon completion.

DTE Energy's construction of the battery energy storage system (BESS) commenced in 2024, following MPSC approval for the Trenton project. The utility had originally partnered with Powin for the BESS technology but shifted to LG Energy Solution after experiencing delays and a contract termination with Powin in June 2025. This strategic pivot is aimed at achieving a cost reduction of over US$30 million for the project.

The financing for Related Digital's data centre, which will utilize DTE Energy's power, involves equity from Related Digital and funds associated with Blackstone, alongside long-term fixed-rate debt anchored by Pacific Investment Management Company (PIMCO). The project is anticipated to generate significant economic benefits, including job creation and tax revenue for local communities, while also reinforcing DTE Energy's commitment to renewable energy solutions.

Recent News 5 of 5, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 298 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: City Opera House Partners with DTE Energy for Sustainable Initiatives

TRAVERSE CITY — The City Opera House has named DTE Energy as its first green sponsor to support environmentally conscious operations. As of 24th April 2026, this partnership marks a significant step towards implementing sustainability initiatives within the organization.

The collaboration aims to enhance eco-friendly practices, including the transition to rechargeable systems for microphone packs, which will eliminate the need for disposable batteries. Additionally, the City Opera House is adopting recycled copy paper throughout its operations, encompassing all printed program inserts. Future plans involve installing a bike rack to promote alternative transportation options for both patrons and staff.

Chad Lindsey, the executive director of the City Opera House, emphasized the importance of this partnership, stating, “DTE Energy's partnership helps us make thoughtful, earth-conscious decisions that align with our values as a historic community anchor. Together, we're ensuring that our operations reflect not only artistic excellence but environmental responsibility.” This sentiment underscores the venue's commitment to integrating sustainability into its core mission.

Henry Decker, vice president of gas sales and supply at DTE Energy, reiterated the company's dedication to sustainability, remarking, “We look forward to supporting the City Opera House as they continue to make a meaningful difference for the environment and for the people they serve.” DTE Energy is known for its initiatives that help customers reduce their carbon footprints, such as the CleanVision Natural Gas Balance program, which balances carbon emissions from natural gas use by supporting Michigan forest management and the development of renewable natural gas.

The City Opera House serves as a cultural hub for NorthernMichigan and remains committed to reducing its environmental footprint. The collaboration with DTE Energy is a promising step towards fostering a more sustainable future, reflecting a growing trend among cultural institutions to prioritize environmental responsibility alongside their artistic missions.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 72 words

Operational Veteran Joi Harris Elected CEO to Navigate Decarbonization and Grid Challenges

Joi Harris, formerly President and COO, has been appointed CEO and President of DTE Energy, succeeding Jerry Norcia. Her extensive operational experience is expected to guide the company through its decarbonization strategy and grid modernization challenges. While her appointment is viewed as a move for continuity, she faces significant regulatory scrutiny over rates and service quality, and her performance will be measured by her ability to meet growth targets amidst these pressures.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 70 words

DTE Energy Outperforms Q3 Expectations Despite Revenue Headwinds, Reaffirms Guidance

DTE Energy reported Q3 2025 operating earnings of $2.25 per share, surpassing estimates despite a nearly $1 billion revenue shortfall caused by lower fuel cost pass-throughs. The earnings beat was driven by cost management and tax credit timing in the DTE Electric segment. Management reaffirmed its full-year guidance, signaling financial stability, but also noted that future growth will depend more on industrial and data center demand rather than residential load.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

MPSC Rejection of Saline Data Center Contract Signals Regulatory Headwinds for Tech Load Growth

DTE Energy's plan to build a major data center in Saline Township was blocked after the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) failed to approve a special contract before a key deadline. The project was central to DTE's strategy of offsetting stagnant residential demand. The rejection, based on concerns about cost-shifting to residential customers and grid stability, sets a challenging precedent for future large-scale industrial projects and introduces significant execution risk to DTE's growth thesis.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 63 words

Regulator Authorizes $217 Million Rate Increase, Capping Revenue Below Utility Request

The MPSC approved a $217.4 million rate increase for DTE Electric, which was only 48% of the company's original request. While the revenue supports grid modernization and near-term financials, the significant reduction signals a tightening regulatory environment. This outcome forces DTE to file for rate increases more frequently to recover its infrastructure investments, a strategy that risks creating friction with regulators and consumers.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 67 words

DTE Files $574 Million Rate Case Amidst Heightened Political Scrutiny

Shortly after its last rate adjustment, DTE Energy filed for another increase, requesting $574.1 million to fund a $4.4 billion investment in grid hardening and cleaner energy. The request has drawn immediate political opposition, notably from the Michigan Attorney General. The outcome is critical for DTE's ability to earn its authorized return and execute its reliability improvement strategy, but a significant disallowance could threaten its growth targets.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 64 words

Investment Plan Expanded by $6.5 Billion to Support Grid Modernization and Load Growth

DTE Energy has increased its five-year capital investment plan by $6.5 billion to accelerate grid modernization and expand renewable generation. The expansion is intended to support a 6-8% annual EPS growth rate, driven by projected load increases from electrification and data centers. However, this large-scale plan faces significant execution risk, including the need for regulatory approval for cost recovery and potential supply chain constraints.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 58 words

Commissioning of Polaris Solar Park Advances Voluntary Green Power Strategy

DTE Energy has brought the 100-megawatt Polaris Solar Park into commercial operation. As a key part of its MIGreenPower program, the facility allows corporate customers to meet their ESG goals. The project's success demonstrates DTE's ability to execute utility-scale solar developments, although the scalability of this model faces future challenges related to land availability and local community opposition.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 63 words

Construction Begins on Dedicated Solar Array for Ford Motor Company

DTE has started construction on a utility-scale solar park dedicated to supplying power to Ford Motor Company's EV manufacturing operations. This project secures long-term revenue from a major industrial partner and mitigates the risk of load defection. While it reinforces DTE's "energy-as-a-service" model, it also ties a portion of the utility's performance to the cyclical automotive industry and Ford's specific EV strategy success.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 65 words

Conversion of Belle River Plant Commences, Pivoting Coal Assets to Peaker Role

DTE has begun converting a coal-fired unit at its Belle River Power Plant to a natural gas peaker plant. The $154 million project is a strategic step to ensure grid reliability as the company retires coal plants and integrates more renewables. This conversion repurposes an existing asset to maintain resource adequacy cost-effectively, though the long-term viability of gas assets remains subject to future environmental policy.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 68 words

Advanced Negotiations for 3 GW Data Center Load Define Long-Term Growth Thesis

DTE Energy is in advanced negotiations to supply approximately 3 GW of power to prospective data center clients, a volume that could transform its commercial load profile. This potential demand is central to the company's long-term growth strategy. However, realizing this pipeline is contingent on overcoming significant regulatory hurdles related to cost allocation and ensuring sufficient generation capacity to serve such a large load without compromising grid reliability.

ECO NEWS 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 322 words

May 7, 2026: AEP's $78 Billion Capital Plan Establishes Regulatory Template for Large-Load Contracts, Reinforcing DTE Energy's Michigan Data Center Strategy

According to industry reports, American Electric Power lifted its five-year capital plan to $78 billion from $72 billion on May 5, 2026, citing accelerating contracted data center demand through 2030. The $6 billion expansion funds new transmission projects in SPP ($1.6 billion) and PJM ($1.9 billion), plus new gas-fired generation in Indiana, while contracted load rose to 63 GW with nearly 90% tied to hyperscalers.

Operational metrics indicate that AEP Texas alone accounts for 41 GW of large-load commitments, supported by the upcoming rollout of Texas Senate Bill 6. The plan reinforces a structural shift across US investor-owned utilities, where regulated rate base is expanding sharply to serve hyperscale and AI-driven load. AEP also raised its long-term operating earnings CAGR target to greater than 9% through 2030 and identified over $10 billion in additional line-of-sight investments beyond the current plan.

Impact on DTE Energy: DTE

Following this news, DTE Energy faces reinforced strategic momentum as AEP validates the regulated-utility data center thesis in a state-by-state manner that mirrors DTE's own Michigan opportunity. DTE signed its first data center contract for 1.4 GW of capacity for a project by OpenAI, Oracle, and Related Digital, with DTE supplying all the power from existing resources, including energy storage systems. AEP's disclosure of cost offsets for existing customers and its treatment of large-load contracts under regulated frameworks provides a directly applicable template that DTE can reference before the Michigan Public Service Commission as it pursues additional contracts and updates its capital plan.

Operationally, DTE faces the same supply chain pressure on gas turbines, transformers, and transmission EPC capacity that AEP encounters, and competition for these resources tightens with each new utility plan announcement. DTE's positioning to convert its industrial-heavy Michigan footprint into a hyperscale supply region strengthens with each peer disclosure that reinforces the durability of contracted load.

May 12 2026: DTE Energy Secures Board Approval Amidst Growing Investor Confidence

DTE Energy Co. (NYSE:DTE) reported that shareholders elected all 13 nominated directors to its board and approved other proposals at the company’s annual meeting held on 13th May 2026. This decisive support reflects the confidence investors have in DTE Energy's governance and strategic direction.

Among those elected to serve one-year terms expiring in 2027 are Nicholas K. Akins, David A. Brandon, and Deborah L. Byers, among others. Each nominee received significant backing, with at least 149 million votes in favor, while the number of votes withheld ranged from approximately 482,000 to over 6.4 million. The strong shareholder support comes as DTE Energy maintains its reputation as a reliable dividend payer, having sustained dividend payments for 56 consecutive years, with a current yield of 3.27%.

Shareholders also ratified the appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as DTE Energy’s independent registered public accounting firm for 2026. This proposal garnered 174,185,300 votes in favor and reflects the trust in the company’s financial oversight. Additionally, shareholders approved, on an advisory basis, the compensation of the company’s named executive officers, with 149,823,374 votes in favor, indicating a favorable view of executive performance.

In recent financial updates, DTE Energy reported first-quarter adjusted earnings per share of $1.95, which fell short of the consensus estimate of $2.03, primarily due to timing issues in its Energy Trading segment. Despite this setback, management reaffirmed its guidance and expressed confidence in achieving an 8% or higher earnings per share growth from 2027 through 2030, bolstered by firming hyperscaler contracts. The company has also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.165 per share, payable on 15th July 2026.

Analysts have adjusted their price targets for DTE Energy in light of these developments. Mizuho raised its target to $165 from $155 while maintaining an Outperform rating. BofA Securities also increased its target to $162 from $155, reflecting a Buy rating. Conversely, Jefferies lowered its target to $168 from $170 but retained a Buy rating, citing various factors that reduce risk. These adjustments highlight ongoing confidence in DTE Energy’s growth trajectory.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 7.4% annualized over 10 years. DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $142.43 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 22.1. With an EPS growth rate of 7.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.98 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 10 years (investors prefer using a 10-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Here is how DTE Energy Company's peers compare:

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

value.Title: DTE Energy Company ( NYSE :DTE) in 2nd consecutive rise, quarterly dividends steady; +$1.94 [+1.4%]

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJP-SH43-RWVH-C0J2-00000-00DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) in 2nd consecutive rise, quarterly dividends steady; +$1.94 [+1.4%]2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:38ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) in 2nd consecutive rise, quarterly dividends steady; +$1.94 [+1.4%]

NYSE END-OF-DAY STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

DETROIT, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $142.54) stock price extended its gains on Tuesday, rising an additional 11.0c (0.1%) to close at $142.54.

This brought its 2-day rise to 1.4%.

Cumulative volume in the 2 days was 0.9 times the average 2-day volume of 1.4 million shares. There were 874,958 shares worth $124.7 million traded today.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

Fri May 1, 2026: DTE Energy Unveils 8.4 GW Data Center Growth Potential

DTE Energy Company is poised to capitalize on significant growth in the data center sector, with plans to support a 1.4-GW Oracle data center currently under construction. On the 1st of May, 2026, company officials revealed that they are also in discussions regarding an additional 7 GW of potential data center projects, indicating a robust pipeline that could reshape the utility's operations in the coming years.

During the company's first quarter earnings call, President and CEO Joi Harris noted that DTE Energy is making steady progress in finalizing contractual agreements to support this data center growth. The utility reported operating earnings of $407 million for Q1 2026, a slight decrease from $436 million in the same period of 2025, attributed to fluctuations in energy trading, tax timing, and elevated interest expenses. However, its electric subsidiary, DTE Electric, experienced an increase in operating earnings to $218 million, up from $147 million year-over-year, driven by favorable weather conditions and successful rate implementations.

The Michigan Public Service Commission granted conditional approval for the Oracle contract in December, paving the way for construction and phased load ramp-up over the next several years. Oracle's investment includes covering the full cost of its energy and capacity needs, supported by existing capabilities and planned energy storage. In addition, DTE Energy is awaiting approval for a 1-GW Google data center project, which is expected to come online by the end of 2028, bolstered by a mix of renewable generation and energy storage solutions.

Meeting the energy demands of Google could drive an estimated $5 billion in incremental investments in generation and storage through 2032. Harris emphasized that these investments are structured to protect existing customers while providing affordability benefits. The proposed 20-year power supply agreement with Google includes provisions designed to ensure that current customers are not adversely affected by the utility's expansion efforts.

Beyond the Oracle and Google projects, DTE Energy is in advanced discussions concerning additional data center projects that could add 2 GW of incremental load, with even more potential projects identified in the pipeline. As the utility continues to enhance its electric reliability—evidenced by a recent audit and a commitment to modernize its distribution system—DTE Energy remains confident in its ability to meet its long-term operating earnings per share growth target of 6-8% through 2030, supported by ongoing investments and strategic partnerships in the evolving data center landscape.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 359 words

Tue Apr 28, 2026: DTE Energy's Rate Freeze Plan Tied to Oracle's Data Centre Development

US utility DTE Energy has announced a plan to pause customer rate increases for two years, contingent upon the successful development of a US$16 billion data centre campus being constructed for tech giant Oracle. This announcement comes as DTE Energy prepares to file for regulatory approval with the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) on 28th April 2026, aiming for a rate freeze that extends through at least 2028.

Joi Harris, president and CEO of DTE Energy, emphasized the importance of the data centre project, stating, 'As long as the first data centre project we're supporting comes online as planned by the end of 2027 and we're able to receive other regulatory approvals, we will refrain from filing another rate request until at least 2028—providing customers two years without an increase in rates after the current request is complete.'

The two data centre contracts associated with the Oracle project are projected to contribute approximately US$9 billion to enhancements in DTE's electric system by 2045, thereby alleviating cost pressures on other customers. The utility's upcoming request for US$474.3 million will support significant investments in the electric grid and power generation, including the conversion of the Belle River Power Plant from coal to natural gas and the establishment of the 220MW/880MWh Trenton Channel Energy Center, which is expected to become the largest stand-alone battery energy storage facility in the Great Lakes region upon completion.

DTE Energy's construction of the battery energy storage system (BESS) commenced in 2024, following MPSC approval for the Trenton project. The utility had originally partnered with Powin for the BESS technology but shifted to LG Energy Solution after experiencing delays and a contract termination with Powin in June 2025. This strategic pivot is aimed at achieving a cost reduction of over US$30 million for the project.

The financing for Related Digital's data centre, which will utilize DTE Energy's power, involves equity from Related Digital and funds associated with Blackstone, alongside long-term fixed-rate debt anchored by Pacific Investment Management Company (PIMCO). The project is anticipated to generate significant economic benefits, including job creation and tax revenue for local communities, while also reinforcing DTE Energy's commitment to renewable energy solutions.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 298 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: City Opera House Partners with DTE Energy for Sustainable Initiatives

TRAVERSE CITY — The City Opera House has named DTE Energy as its first green sponsor to support environmentally conscious operations. As of 24th April 2026, this partnership marks a significant step towards implementing sustainability initiatives within the organization.

The collaboration aims to enhance eco-friendly practices, including the transition to rechargeable systems for microphone packs, which will eliminate the need for disposable batteries. Additionally, the City Opera House is adopting recycled copy paper throughout its operations, encompassing all printed program inserts. Future plans involve installing a bike rack to promote alternative transportation options for both patrons and staff.

Chad Lindsey, the executive director of the City Opera House, emphasized the importance of this partnership, stating, “DTE Energy's partnership helps us make thoughtful, earth-conscious decisions that align with our values as a historic community anchor. Together, we're ensuring that our operations reflect not only artistic excellence but environmental responsibility.” This sentiment underscores the venue's commitment to integrating sustainability into its core mission.

Henry Decker, vice president of gas sales and supply at DTE Energy, reiterated the company's dedication to sustainability, remarking, “We look forward to supporting the City Opera House as they continue to make a meaningful difference for the environment and for the people they serve.” DTE Energy is known for its initiatives that help customers reduce their carbon footprints, such as the CleanVision Natural Gas Balance program, which balances carbon emissions from natural gas use by supporting Michigan forest management and the development of renewable natural gas.

The City Opera House serves as a cultural hub for NorthernMichigan and remains committed to reducing its environmental footprint. The collaboration with DTE Energy is a promising step towards fostering a more sustainable future, reflecting a growing trend among cultural institutions to prioritize environmental responsibility alongside their artistic missions.

ECO NEWS 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 322 words

May 7, 2026: AEP's $78 Billion Capital Plan Establishes Regulatory Template for Large-Load Contracts, Reinforcing DTE Energy's Michigan Data Center Strategy

According to industry reports, American Electric Power lifted its five-year capital plan to $78 billion from $72 billion on May 5, 2026, citing accelerating contracted data center demand through 2030. The $6 billion expansion funds new transmission projects in SPP ($1.6 billion) and PJM ($1.9 billion), plus new gas-fired generation in Indiana, while contracted load rose to 63 GW with nearly 90% tied to hyperscalers.

Operational metrics indicate that AEP Texas alone accounts for 41 GW of large-load commitments, supported by the upcoming rollout of Texas Senate Bill 6. The plan reinforces a structural shift across US investor-owned utilities, where regulated rate base is expanding sharply to serve hyperscale and AI-driven load. AEP also raised its long-term operating earnings CAGR target to greater than 9% through 2030 and identified over $10 billion in additional line-of-sight investments beyond the current plan.

Impact on DTE Energy: DTE

Following this news, DTE Energy faces reinforced strategic momentum as AEP validates the regulated-utility data center thesis in a state-by-state manner that mirrors DTE's own Michigan opportunity. DTE signed its first data center contract for 1.4 GW of capacity for a project by OpenAI, Oracle, and Related Digital, with DTE supplying all the power from existing resources, including energy storage systems. AEP's disclosure of cost offsets for existing customers and its treatment of large-load contracts under regulated frameworks provides a directly applicable template that DTE can reference before the Michigan Public Service Commission as it pursues additional contracts and updates its capital plan.

Operationally, DTE faces the same supply chain pressure on gas turbines, transformers, and transmission EPC capacity that AEP encounters, and competition for these resources tightens with each new utility plan announcement. DTE's positioning to convert its industrial-heavy Michigan footprint into a hyperscale supply region strengthens with each peer disclosure that reinforces the durability of contracted load.

NYSE END-OF-DAY STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

DETROIT, Monday, May 11 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $142.42) stock price lifted 1.3% but fell short of the 12-day Exponential Moving Average [EMA] of $144.47.

Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 7.9 points (0.1%) in the day, the relative price increase was 1.2%.

The stock last traded at $142.42.

There were 1,018,587 shares worth $145.1 million traded today; the volume was 0.7 times the average daily trading volume of 1.4 million shares.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

Fri May 1, 2026: DTE Energy Unveils 8.4 GW Data Center Growth Potential

DTE Energy Company is poised to capitalize on significant growth in the data center sector, with plans to support a 1.4-GW Oracle data center currently under construction. On the 1st of May, 2026, company officials revealed that they are also in discussions regarding an additional 7 GW of potential data center projects, indicating a robust pipeline that could reshape the utility's operations in the coming years.

During the company's first quarter earnings call, President and CEO Joi Harris noted that DTE Energy is making steady progress in finalizing contractual agreements to support this data center growth. The utility reported operating earnings of $407 million for Q1 2026, a slight decrease from $436 million in the same period of 2025, attributed to fluctuations in energy trading, tax timing, and elevated interest expenses. However, its electric subsidiary, DTE Electric, experienced an increase in operating earnings to $218 million, up from $147 million year-over-year, driven by favorable weather conditions and successful rate implementations.

The Michigan Public Service Commission granted conditional approval for the Oracle contract in December, paving the way for construction and phased load ramp-up over the next several years. Oracle's investment includes covering the full cost of its energy and capacity needs, supported by existing capabilities and planned energy storage. In addition, DTE Energy is awaiting approval for a 1-GW Google data center project, which is expected to come online by the end of 2028, bolstered by a mix of renewable generation and energy storage solutions.

Meeting the energy demands of Google could drive an estimated $5 billion in incremental investments in generation and storage through 2032. Harris emphasized that these investments are structured to protect existing customers while providing affordability benefits. The proposed 20-year power supply agreement with Google includes provisions designed to ensure that current customers are not adversely affected by the utility's expansion efforts.

Beyond the Oracle and Google projects, DTE Energy is in advanced discussions concerning additional data center projects that could add 2 GW of incremental load, with even more potential projects identified in the pipeline. As the utility continues to enhance its electric reliability—evidenced by a recent audit and a commitment to modernize its distribution system—DTE Energy remains confident in its ability to meet its long-term operating earnings per share growth target of 6-8% through 2030, supported by ongoing investments and strategic partnerships in the evolving data center landscape.

Tue Apr 28, 2026: DTE Energy's Rate Freeze Plan Tied to Oracle's Data Centre Development

US utility DTE Energy has announced a plan to pause customer rate increases for two years, contingent upon the successful development of a US$16 billion data centre campus being constructed for tech giant Oracle. This announcement comes as DTE Energy prepares to file for regulatory approval with the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) on 28th April 2026, aiming for a rate freeze that extends through at least 2028.

Joi Harris, president and CEO of DTE Energy, emphasized the importance of the data centre project, stating, 'As long as the first data centre project we're supporting comes online as planned by the end of 2027 and we're able to receive other regulatory approvals, we will refrain from filing another rate request until at least 2028—providing customers two years without an increase in rates after the current request is complete.'

The two data centre contracts associated with the Oracle project are projected to contribute approximately US$9 billion to enhancements in DTE's electric system by 2045, thereby alleviating cost pressures on other customers. The utility's upcoming request for US$474.3 million will support significant investments in the electric grid and power generation, including the conversion of the Belle River Power Plant from coal to natural gas and the establishment of the 220MW/880MWh Trenton Channel Energy Center, which is expected to become the largest stand-alone battery energy storage facility in the Great Lakes region upon completion.

DTE Energy's construction of the battery energy storage system (BESS) commenced in 2024, following MPSC approval for the Trenton project. The utility had originally partnered with Powin for the BESS technology but shifted to LG Energy Solution after experiencing delays and a contract termination with Powin in June 2025. This strategic pivot is aimed at achieving a cost reduction of over US$30 million for the project.

The financing for Related Digital's data centre, which will utilize DTE Energy's power, involves equity from Related Digital and funds associated with Blackstone, alongside long-term fixed-rate debt anchored by Pacific Investment Management Company (PIMCO). The project is anticipated to generate significant economic benefits, including job creation and tax revenue for local communities, while also reinforcing DTE Energy's commitment to renewable energy solutions.

Thu Apr 23, 2026: City Opera House Partners with DTE Energy for Sustainable Initiatives

TRAVERSE CITY — The City Opera House has named DTE Energy as its first green sponsor to support environmentally conscious operations. As of 24th April 2026, this partnership marks a significant step towards implementing sustainability initiatives within the organization.

The collaboration aims to enhance eco-friendly practices, including the transition to rechargeable systems for microphone packs, which will eliminate the need for disposable batteries. Additionally, the City Opera House is adopting recycled copy paper throughout its operations, encompassing all printed program inserts. Future plans involve installing a bike rack to promote alternative transportation options for both patrons and staff.

Chad Lindsey, the executive director of the City Opera House, emphasized the importance of this partnership, stating, “DTE Energy's partnership helps us make thoughtful, earth-conscious decisions that align with our values as a historic community anchor. Together, we're ensuring that our operations reflect not only artistic excellence but environmental responsibility.” This sentiment underscores the venue's commitment to integrating sustainability into its core mission.

Henry Decker, vice president of gas sales and supply at DTE Energy, reiterated the company's dedication to sustainability, remarking, “We look forward to supporting the City Opera House as they continue to make a meaningful difference for the environment and for the people they serve.” DTE Energy is known for its initiatives that help customers reduce their carbon footprints, such as the CleanVision Natural Gas Balance program, which balances carbon emissions from natural gas use by supporting Michigan forest management and the development of renewable natural gas.

The City Opera House serves as a cultural hub for Northern Michigan and remains committed to reducing its environmental footprint. The collaboration with DTE Energy is a promising step towards fostering a more sustainable future, reflecting a growing trend among cultural institutions to prioritize environmental responsibility alongside their artistic missions.

May 7, 2026: AEP's $78 Billion Capital Plan Establishes Regulatory Template for Large-Load Contracts, Reinforcing DTE Energy's Michigan Data Center Strategy

According to industry reports, American Electric Power lifted its five-year capital plan to $78 billion from $72 billion on May 5, 2026, citing accelerating contracted data center demand through 2030. The $6 billion expansion funds new transmission projects in SPP ($1.6 billion) and PJM ($1.9 billion), plus new gas-fired generation in Indiana, while contracted load rose to 63 GW with nearly 90% tied to hyperscalers.

Operational metrics indicate that AEP Texas alone accounts for 41 GW of large-load commitments, supported by the upcoming rollout of Texas Senate Bill 6. The plan reinforces a structural shift across US investor-owned utilities, where regulated rate base is expanding sharply to serve hyperscale and AI-driven load. AEP also raised its long-term operating earnings CAGR target to greater than 9% through 2030 and identified over $10 billion in additional line-of-sight investments beyond the current plan.

Impact on DTE Energy: DTE

Following this news, DTE Energy faces reinforced strategic momentum as AEP validates the regulated-utility data center thesis in a state-by-state manner that mirrors DTE's own Michigan opportunity. DTE signed its first data center contract for 1.4 GW of capacity for a project by OpenAI, Oracle, and Related Digital, with DTE supplying all the power from existing resources, including energy storage systems. AEP's disclosure of cost offsets for existing customers and its treatment of large-load contracts under regulated frameworks provides a directly applicable template that DTE can reference before the Michigan Public Service Commission as it pursues additional contracts and updates its capital plan.

Operationally, DTE faces the same supply chain pressure on gas turbines, transformers, and transmission EPC capacity that AEP encounters, and competition for these resources tightens with each new utility plan announcement. DTE's positioning to convert its industrial-heavy Michigan footprint into a hyperscale supply region strengthens with each peer disclosure that reinforces the durability of contracted load.
