SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS & PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 359 words

Tue Apr 28, 2026: DTE Energy's Rate Freeze Plan Tied to Oracle's Data Centre Development

US utility DTE Energy has announced a plan to pause customer rate increases for two years, contingent upon the successful development of a US$16 billion data centre campus being constructed for tech giant Oracle. This announcement comes as DTE Energy prepares to file for regulatory approval with the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) on 28th April 2026, aiming for a rate freeze that extends through at least 2028.

Joi Harris, president and CEO of DTE Energy, emphasized the importance of the data centre project, stating, 'As long as the first data centre project we're supporting comes online as planned by the end of 2027 and we're able to receive other regulatory approvals, we will refrain from filing another rate request until at least 2028—providing customers two years without an increase in rates after the current request is complete.'

The two data centre contracts associated with the Oracle project are projected to contribute approximately US$9 billion to enhancements in DTE's electric system by 2045, thereby alleviating cost pressures on other customers. The utility's upcoming request for US$474.3 million will support significant investments in the electric grid and power generation, including the conversion of the Belle River Power Plant from coal to natural gas and the establishment of the 220MW/880MWh Trenton Channel Energy Center, which is expected to become the largest stand-alone battery energy storage facility in the Great Lakes region upon completion.

DTE Energy's construction of the battery energy storage system (BESS) commenced in 2024, following MPSC approval for the Trenton project. The utility had originally partnered with Powin for the BESS technology but shifted to LG Energy Solution after experiencing delays and a contract termination with Powin in June 2025. This strategic pivot is aimed at achieving a cost reduction of over US$30 million for the project.

The financing for Related Digital's data centre, which will utilize DTE Energy's power, involves equity from Related Digital and funds associated with Blackstone, alongside long-term fixed-rate debt anchored by Pacific Investment Management Company (PIMCO). The project is anticipated to generate significant economic benefits, including job creation and tax revenue for local communities, while also reinforcing DTE Energy's commitment to renewable energy solutions.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 298 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: City Opera House Partners with DTE Energy for Sustainable Initiatives

TRAVERSE CITY — The City Opera House has named DTE Energy as its first green sponsor to support environmentally conscious operations. As of 24th April 2026, this partnership marks a significant step towards implementing sustainability initiatives within the organization.

The collaboration aims to enhance eco-friendly practices, including the transition to rechargeable systems for microphone packs, which will eliminate the need for disposable batteries. Additionally, the City Opera House is adopting recycled copy paper throughout its operations, encompassing all printed program inserts. Future plans involve installing a bike rack to promote alternative transportation options for both patrons and staff.

Chad Lindsey, the executive director of the City Opera House, emphasized the importance of this partnership, stating, “DTE Energy's partnership helps us make thoughtful, earth-conscious decisions that align with our values as a historic community anchor. Together, we're ensuring that our operations reflect not only artistic excellence but environmental responsibility.” This sentiment underscores the venue's commitment to integrating sustainability into its core mission.

Henry Decker, vice president of gas sales and supply at DTE Energy, reiterated the company's dedication to sustainability, remarking, “We look forward to supporting the City Opera House as they continue to make a meaningful difference for the environment and for the people they serve.” DTE Energy is known for its initiatives that help customers reduce their carbon footprints, such as the CleanVision Natural Gas Balance program, which balances carbon emissions from natural gas use by supporting Michigan forest management and the development of renewable natural gas.

The City Opera House serves as a cultural hub for Northern Michigan and remains committed to reducing its environmental footprint. The collaboration with DTE Energy is a promising step towards fostering a more sustainable future, reflecting a growing trend among cultural institutions to prioritize environmental responsibility alongside their artistic missions.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 72 words

Operational Veteran Joi Harris Elected CEO to Navigate Decarbonization and Grid Challenges

Joi Harris, formerly President and COO, has been appointed CEO and President of DTE Energy, succeeding Jerry Norcia. Her extensive operational experience is expected to guide the company through its decarbonization strategy and grid modernization challenges. While her appointment is viewed as a move for continuity, she faces significant regulatory scrutiny over rates and service quality, and her performance will be measured by her ability to meet growth targets amidst these pressures.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 70 words

DTE Energy Outperforms Q3 Expectations Despite Revenue Headwinds, Reaffirms Guidance

DTE Energy reported Q3 2025 operating earnings of $2.25 per share, surpassing estimates despite a nearly $1 billion revenue shortfall caused by lower fuel cost pass-throughs. The earnings beat was driven by cost management and tax credit timing in the DTE Electric segment. Management reaffirmed its full-year guidance, signaling financial stability, but also noted that future growth will depend more on industrial and data center demand rather than residential load.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

MPSC Rejection of Saline Data Center Contract Signals Regulatory Headwinds for Tech Load Growth

DTE Energy's plan to build a major data center in Saline Township was blocked after the Michigan Public Service Commission (MPSC) failed to approve a special contract before a key deadline. The project was central to DTE's strategy of offsetting stagnant residential demand. The rejection, based on concerns about cost-shifting to residential customers and grid stability, sets a challenging precedent for future large-scale industrial projects and introduces significant execution risk to DTE's growth thesis.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 63 words

Regulator Authorizes $217 Million Rate Increase, Capping Revenue Below Utility Request

The MPSC approved a $217.4 million rate increase for DTE Electric, which was only 48% of the company's original request. While the revenue supports grid modernization and near-term financials, the significant reduction signals a tightening regulatory environment. This outcome forces DTE to file for rate increases more frequently to recover its infrastructure investments, a strategy that risks creating friction with regulators and consumers.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 67 words

DTE Files $574 Million Rate Case Amidst Heightened Political Scrutiny

Shortly after its last rate adjustment, DTE Energy filed for another increase, requesting $574.1 million to fund a $4.4 billion investment in grid hardening and cleaner energy. The request has drawn immediate political opposition, notably from the Michigan Attorney General. The outcome is critical for DTE's ability to earn its authorized return and execute its reliability improvement strategy, but a significant disallowance could threaten its growth targets.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 64 words

Investment Plan Expanded by $6.5 Billion to Support Grid Modernization and Load Growth

DTE Energy has increased its five-year capital investment plan by $6.5 billion to accelerate grid modernization and expand renewable generation. The expansion is intended to support a 6-8% annual EPS growth rate, driven by projected load increases from electrification and data centers. However, this large-scale plan faces significant execution risk, including the need for regulatory approval for cost recovery and potential supply chain constraints.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 58 words

Commissioning of Polaris Solar Park Advances Voluntary Green Power Strategy

DTE Energy has brought the 100-megawatt Polaris Solar Park into commercial operation. As a key part of its MIGreenPower program, the facility allows corporate customers to meet their ESG goals. The project's success demonstrates DTE's ability to execute utility-scale solar developments, although the scalability of this model faces future challenges related to land availability and local community opposition.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 63 words

Construction Begins on Dedicated Solar Array for Ford Motor Company

DTE has started construction on a utility-scale solar park dedicated to supplying power to Ford Motor Company's EV manufacturing operations. This project secures long-term revenue from a major industrial partner and mitigates the risk of load defection. While it reinforces DTE's "energy-as-a-service" model, it also ties a portion of the utility's performance to the cyclical automotive industry and Ford's specific EV strategy success.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 65 words

Conversion of Belle River Plant Commences, Pivoting Coal Assets to Peaker Role

DTE has begun converting a coal-fired unit at its Belle River Power Plant to a natural gas peaker plant. The $154 million project is a strategic step to ensure grid reliability as the company retires coal plants and integrates more renewables. This conversion repurposes an existing asset to maintain resource adequacy cost-effectively, though the long-term viability of gas assets remains subject to future environmental policy.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 68 words

Advanced Negotiations for 3 GW Data Center Load Define Long-Term Growth Thesis

DTE Energy is in advanced negotiations to supply approximately 3 GW of power to prospective data center clients, a volume that could transform its commercial load profile. This potential demand is central to the company's long-term growth strategy. However, realizing this pipeline is contingent on overcoming significant regulatory hurdles related to cost allocation and ensuring sufficient generation capacity to serve such a large load without compromising grid reliability.

SECTION 4 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 01 2026: Mizuho Adjusts DTE Energy Price Target Amid Hyperscaler Expansion

Mizuho Securities has raised its price target on DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) from $155 to $165, maintaining an Outperform rating as the company continues to capitalize on growth opportunities in the hyperscaler market. This update was published on 4th May 2026, reflecting Mizuho's confidence in DTE Energy's strategic direction and market positioning.

In its latest earnings report, DTE Energy posted first-quarter adjusted earnings per share of $1.95, which fell short of the consensus estimate of $2.03. The shortfall was attributed to timing issues in the Energy Trading segment, which management anticipates will normalize later in the year. Despite this, DTE Energy has reaffirmed its guidance, projecting an impressive 8% or higher growth in earnings per share from 2027 through 2030 as contracts with hyperscalers become more established.

DTE Energy has also proposed a mechanism to pause electric rate case filings until at least 2028, contingent on the ramp-up of Oracle's operations. The company is set to file an integrated resource plan in the third quarter, which is expected to solidify another approximately 2 gigawatts of late-stage negotiations. Notably, the Michigan staff has expressed support for the expansion of the Integrated Resource Model (IRM), and the commission has welcomed the recently secured contract with Google.

In addition to Mizuho's revision, several other analyst firms have adjusted their price targets for DTE Energy, reflecting a growing optimism about the company's future. BofA Securities has raised its target to $162 while maintaining a Buy rating, and Jefferies has upped its target to $170, also with a Buy recommendation, citing DTE's growth in the data center segment. Ladenburg Thalmann has set its target at $165.50, highlighting a significant Primary Supply Agreement with Google for a 1 gigawatt data center project, which involves deploying substantial battery storage and solar generation capacity.

Furthermore, UBS has included DTE Energy in its Global High-Quality Dividend Stock List, recognizing the company as a dependable dividend payer. These developments indicate a robust outlook for DTE Energy, driven by strategic initiatives and a solid financial framework that analysts believe will enable sustained growth in the coming years.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 01 2026: DTE Energy Unveils 8.4 GW Data Center Growth Potential

DTE Energy Company is poised to capitalize on significant growth in the data center sector, with plans to support a 1.4-GW Oracle data center currently under construction. On the 1st of May, 2026, company officials revealed that they are also in discussions regarding an additional 7 GW of potential data center projects, indicating a robust pipeline that could reshape the utility's operations in the coming years.

During the company’s first quarter earnings call, President and CEO Joi Harris noted that DTE Energy is making steady progress in finalizing contractual agreements to support this data center growth. The utility reported operating earnings of $407 million for Q1 2026, a slight decrease from $436 million in the same period of 2025, attributed to fluctuations in energy trading, tax timing, and elevated interest expenses. However, its electric subsidiary, DTE Electric, experienced an increase in operating earnings to $218 million, up from $147 million year-over-year, driven by favorable weather conditions and successful rate implementations.

The Michigan Public Service Commission granted conditional approval for the Oracle contract in December, paving the way for construction and phased load ramp-up over the next several years. Oracle’s investment includes covering the full cost of its energy and capacity needs, supported by existing capabilities and planned energy storage. In addition, DTE Energy is awaiting approval for a 1-GW Google data center project, which is expected to come online by the end of 2028, bolstered by a mix of renewable generation and energy storage solutions.

Meeting the energy demands of Google could drive an estimated $5 billion in incremental investments in generation and storage through 2032. Harris emphasized that these investments are structured to protect existing customers while providing affordability benefits. The proposed 20-year power supply agreement with Google includes provisions designed to ensure that current customers are not adversely affected by the utility's expansion efforts.

Beyond the Oracle and Google projects, DTE Energy is in advanced discussions concerning additional data center projects that could add 2 GW of incremental load, with even more potential projects identified in the pipeline. As the utility continues to enhance its electric reliability—evidenced by a recent audit and a commitment to modernize its distribution system—DTE Energy remains confident in its ability to meet its long-term operating earnings per share growth target of 6-8% through 2030, supported by ongoing investments and strategic partnerships in the evolving data center landscape.

value.Title: April 30, 2026: TENASKA & PEERS: INDUSTRY NEWS DIGEST

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-48N3-S0MJ-D289-00000-00April 30, 2026: TENASKA & PEERS: INDUSTRY NEWS DIGEST2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:51:00ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 30, 2026: TENASKA & PEERS: INDUSTRY NEWS DIGEST

On April 21, 2026, Bobwhite Energy Storage, an affiliate of Tenaska, finalized a 20-year agreement with the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) for a 225-megawatt battery storage system in Hawkins County, East Tennessee. This $300 million project is expected to create up to 75 jobs during peak construction. Monika Beckner, TVA Vice President of Power Supply & Fuels, stated the project will enhance regional energy security, while John Hejkal, Vice President of Origination & Development at Tenaska, noted it exemplifies the company's approach to developing high-value energy projects.

In Fluvanna County, the Board of Supervisors voted 4-1 on March 19, 2026, to approve a special use permit for Tenaska's Expedition Generation power plant. The approval overturned an earlier Planning Commission decision and included concessions such as stricter noise standards and a $5 million Good Neighbor Fund. The plant is projected to open as early as 2031 following a state permitting process expected to take approximately two years.

Shell plc announced a definitive agreement on April 27, 2026, to acquire Canadian energy company ARC Resources Ltd. in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately CAD$22 billion. ARC shareholders will receive 0.40247 of a Shell share and CAD$8.20 in cash per ARC share. The deal adds 1.5 million net acres and 2 billion barrels of oil-equivalent reserves to Shell's portfolio. ARC CEO Terry Anderson emphasized the transaction unlocks significant shareholder value, while ARC Board Chair Hal Kvisle highlighted the alignment of core values and long-term value creation.

Additionally, on April 23, 2026, CleanMax Enviro Energy Solutions Limited announced a strategic collaboration to supply hybrid renewable power to Shell's operations in India. The 30-megawatt capacity project includes a 16.83-megawatt facility in Gujarat for Shell's LNG terminal and a 13.2-megawatt facility in Karnataka for the Shell Technology Centre. CleanMax Managing Director Kuldeep Jain stated the collaboration supports reducing carbon emissions, while Mansi Madan Tripathy, Chairperson of Shell Group of Companies India, noted it reflects a commitment to cleaner energy systems.

American Electric Power held its annual meeting on April 28, 2026, where shareholders approved an increase in authorized common shares from 600 million to 900 million and re-elected the full slate of 10 directors. CEO William Fehrman reported 2025 operating earnings of $5.97 per share and highlighted a $72 billion capital plan to address anticipated load growth driven by advancements in artificial intelligence. Sara Martinez Tucker, the company's lead director, reiterated the board's commitment to providing reliable, affordable power across its regulated footprint.

In separate announcements, American Electric Power declared a quarterly dividend of $0.95 per share on April 28, 2026, and revealed plans on April 13, 2026, to file for additional generation capacity through its Indiana Michigan Power subsidiary to meet rising energy demands linked to the data center boom.

ConocoPhillips dispatched an evaluation team to Venezuela during the week of April 6, 2026, to assess potential oil and gas opportunities, confirming the move on April 13, 2026. The company is evaluating the viability of investments following shifts in the geopolitical landscape, returning to assess a region it had previously exited following the nationalization of oil fields.

ConocoPhillips also marked 45 years of partnership in China on April 9, 2026. Markel H. binette, ConocoPhillips China President, noted the company has played a meaningful role in China's modern energy story since its initial entry. He emphasized that ongoing collaboration will add value to the global portfolio while advancing China's energy supply and security.

Dominion Energy Inc. disclosed a lobbying expenditure of $1.27 million for the first quarter of 2026 in a Lobbying Disclosure Act filing dated April 21, 2026. The filing outlined efforts concerning natural gas, electric utilities, nuclear energy, and offshore wind projects, specifically including the Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind Project and military installation energy modernization.

On April 10, 2026, Dominion Energy announced amendments extending the maturity dates on two revolving credit agreements. The Sustainability Revolving Credit Agreement was extended to April 7, 2029, while the Sixth Amended and Restated Revolving Credit Agreement was extended to April 8, 2031, providing additional flexibility as the company manages a total debt load of $50.2 billion.

Azule Energy, a joint venture between BP and Eni, announced a major oil discovery on April 26, 2026, at the Algaita-01 exploration well offshore Angola. The well is estimated to hold nearly 500 million barrels of crude oil. Azule Energy CEO Joe Murphy expressed confidence that the results reaffirm the high potential of the Lower Congo Basin, a sentiment echoed by Paulino Jeronimo, Chairman and CEO of the Angolan National Agency of Petroleum, Gas and Biofuels.

Furthermore, Spain's competition authority, the CNMC, announced on April 21, 2026, that it had closed its antitrust investigations into BP, Repsol, and Cepsa after finding no evidence that the companies abused their market positions to raise fuel prices. Separately, on April 18, 2026, Norway's $2.2 trillion sovereign wealth fund stated it would support the election of BP Chair Albert Manifold and back management on climate reporting resolutions at the upcoming annual general meeting.

value.Title: Progress Michigan: Advocates Press Senate to Oppose House Farm Bill

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGH-3TR3-RT2D-H022-00000-00Progress Michigan: Advocates Press Senate to Oppose House Farm Bill2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:37ZLexisNexisProgress Michigan: Advocates Press Senate to Oppose House Farm BillTargeted News ServiceLANSING, Michigan

Progress Michigan, an organization that says it holds public officials and government accountable and assist in the promotion of progressive ideas, issued the following news release on May 1, 2026:

* * *

Advocates Press Senate to Oppose House Farm Bill

Polling shows House was correct to get rid of pesticide 'poison pills' due to overwhelming public opposition

*

Advocates held a press conference and poll briefing upon the passage of the Farm, Food and National Security Act (H.R. 7567), also known as the farm bill, in the U.S. House of Representatives.

Multiple Michigan organizations expressed their opposition due to the legislation including billions of dollars in cuts to food assistance, undermining renewable energy programs, stripping funding for conservation programs, creating new environmental loopholes, while allowing taxpayer dollars to be funneled to wealthy corporate commodity farms. This opposition comes as advocacy organizations and farmers sent a letter to Michigan's federal lawmakers this week urging a "nay" vote on the farm bill, while sounding alarm bells back in March when the House Agriculture Committee advanced the legislation.

"While it's important to advance a farm bill, a bad one is worse than not having one," said Bob Thompson, a fifth-generation owner and operator of a centennial farm in Isabella County and president of Michigan Farmers Union. "The farm bill that's being proposed largely continues existing farm safety net programs that really do not match the scale of the current economic crisis that family farmers are facing."

Participants also unveiled polling data from Michigan that found broad support for eliminating the toxic poison bills that were eliminated from the farm bill right before passage. The bill's original language would shield chemical manufacturers for the harms they cause to farmworkers and prevent opposition to local and state governments from enacting their own pesticide labeling requirements, which were eventually defeated in an amendment. This came as the United States Supreme Court held oral arguments this week in Monsanto v. Durnell, which will decide whether pesticide manufacturers like Bayer and Monsanto can be shielded from lawsuits brought against them for health issues tied to their pesticide products.

"The House Republican's farm bill pulls a page from the Project 2025 agenda by weakening environmental safeguards and shielding corporations from the harm they cause to us," said Justin Mendoza, MPH, executive director of Progress Michigan. "We're glad that the poison pills to preempting state and local laws about pesticide application have been stripped from the bill's language, because this move would deny justice to farmers and farmworkers who face the health effects of cancers and chronic illnesses. At the same time, this farm bill as written will not protect the health of rural and urban communities."

Participants on the press call shared what's at stake in Congress and communities across the country while releasing polling data backing up how House leadership is missing the mark with this farm bill and why Senators need to stand firm in the negotiation process.

"Families across this country are already feeling the impact of nearly $187 billion in SNAP cuts enacted through last July's Republican megabill," said Dana Mohammad, statewide coalitions director for Detroit Action. "Now, the so-called Farm, Food, and National Security Act of 2026, would double down on those cuts. It keeps limits on future benefits, adds oppressive work reporting requirements, and imposes new eligibility restrictions that will force millions of families to lose critical support. Let's be clear: SNAP and WIC are not solutions to poverty. They are lifelines. Weakening them will force families into impossible choices between food, rent, medicine, and care for their loved ones."

"The U.S. House of Representatives yesterday passed a Farm Bill that included the so-called and misleadingly titled 'Save Our Bacon' (SOB) Act," said Bee Friedlander, executive committee member of Michiganders for a Just Farming System. "SOB would prohibit state and local governments from regulating standards for raising animals for food outside the state.If you are a large-scale industrial pork producer, often with ties to foreign countries, this is a gift. If you care about animal welfare, small scale farming, rural quality of life, states' rights, the democratic process, federal over-reach, health, this is a loss. However, it faces an uncertain future in the Senate which is seeking to avoid controversial 'poison pill' provisions in the farm bill."

"There are alternative solutions for our food that support regenerative agriculture that would increase security for farmers and people trying to eat and afford food," said Nichole Biber, organizer with Clean Water Action and a tribal citizen of Little Traverse Bay Bands of Odawa Indians. "The Conservation Stewardship Program (CSP) and Environmental Quality Incentives Program (EQIP) in the farm bill are not only not expanded, but they've been decreased at a time when we've been an increasingly insecure place for climate impacts, droughts, and other unknowns."

To learn about the special role that Michigan plays in the farm bill, go to progressmichigan.org/farmbill. To view a recording of today's press conference, go here (https://us02web.zoom.us/rec/share/dOkpT72A5844zynJosgZuRObcsBBWzI3nwQ5RwPDbqneIjLFG013yeA83Cdjmh5C.AgB971CeKcTKhPaC%20%20Passcode:%20SHHUyn*5?link_id=4&can_id=d4b496f6dec1f927ab03d09440f8808c&source=email-release-advocates-press-senate-to-oppose-house-farm-bill&email_referrer=&email_subject=release-advocates-press-senate-to-oppose-house-farm-bill&&).

[View charts in the link at bottom.]
